revision.text: #redirect[[en:Tenno_Shrine]] [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]
天王杉神社は奈良に鎮守し奈良を守護しています。 創建は数百年～千年以上前に天王神社はすでに存在しています。 霊験あらたかであらゆる災害天災から人々を御守護して地域を守っています。

revision.text:
'''天王神社'''（てんのうじんじゃ）は、[[牛頭天王]]を祀る[[神社]]で、日本各地にある。明治の[[神仏分離]]の際、祭神を牛頭天王と習合されていた[[スサノオ]]としたり、古伝に記載された祭神に戻したりした神社もあり、その際に社名を変更した神社も数多い。しかし、現在でも牛頭天王を祭神とする神社が数社ある。 古代より天王天皇系と仏教系天王信仰や天王神社天王杉神社天王寺や天王地名など全国に存在します。
[[Category:祇園神社|*てんのうしんしや]] {{shinto-stub}}

'''天王神社'''（てんのうじんじゃ）は、[[牛頭天王]]を祀る[[神社]]で、日本各地にある。明治の[[神仏分離]]の際、祭神を牛頭天王と習合されていた[[スサノオ]]としたり、古伝に記載された祭神に戻したりした神社もあり、その際に社名を変更した神社も数多い。しかし、現在でも牛頭天王を祭神とする神社が数社ある。 古代より天王天皇系と仏教系天王信仰や天王神社天王杉神社天王寺や天王地名など京都奈良大阪愛知岐阜福井石川新潟佐渡秋田神奈川東京や全国各地に存在します。
[[Category:祇園神社|*てんのうしんしや]] {{shinto-stub}}

'''天王神社'''（てんのうじんじゃ）は、[[牛頭天王]]を祀る[[神社]]で、[[日本]]各地にある。明治の[[神仏分離]]の際、[[祭神]]を[[牛頭天王]]と習合されていた[[スサノオ]]としたり、古伝に記載された祭神に戻したりした神社もあり、その際に社名を変更した神社も数多い。しかし、現在でも[[牛頭天王]]を祭神とする[[神社]]が数社あり[[秩父神社]]も天[[王祭り]]信仰有り。 古代より[[天王]][[天皇]]系と[[仏教]]系[[天王]]信仰や'''天王神社'''[[天王杉神社]]や[[天王寺]]や[[天王]]地名など[[京都]][[奈良]][[大阪]][[愛知]]岐阜福井石川[[新潟]]佐渡[[秋田]]神奈川[[東京]]や全国各地に存在します。
[[Category:祇園神社|*てんのうしんしや]] {{shinto-stub}}

'''天王神社'''（てんのうじんじゃ）は、[[牛頭天王]]を祀る[[神社]]で、[[日本]]各地にある。明治の[[神仏分離]]の際、[[祭神]]を[[牛頭天王]]と習合されていた[[スサノオ]]としたり、古伝に記載された祭神に戻したりした神社もあり、その際に社名を変更した神社も数多い。しかし、現在でも[[牛頭天王]]を祭神とする[[神社]]が数社あり[[秩父神社]]も[[天王祭り]]信仰有り。 古代より[[天王]][[天皇]]系と[[仏教]]系[[天王]]信仰や'''天王神社'''[[天王杉神社]]や[[天王寺]]や[[天王]]地名など[[京都]][[奈良]][[大阪]][[愛知]]岐阜福井石川[[新潟]]佐渡[[秋田]]神奈川[[東京]]や全国各地に存在します。
[[Category:祇園神社|*てんのうしんしや]] {{shinto-stub}}

'''天王神社'''（てんのうじんじゃ）は、[[牛頭天王]]を祀る[[神社]]で、[[日本]]各地にある。明治の[[神仏分離]]の際、[[祭神]]を[[牛頭天王]]と習合されていた[[スサノオ]]としたり、古伝に記載された祭神に戻したりした神社もあり、その際に社名を変更した神社も数多い。しかし、現在でも[[牛頭天王]]を祭神とする[[神社]]が数社あり日本３大[[祇園]]祭りの[[秩父神社]]も[[天王祭り]]信仰有り。 古代より[[天王]][[天皇]]系と[[仏教]]系[[天王]]信仰や'''天王神社'''[[天王杉神社]]や[[天王寺]]や[[天王]]地名など[[京都]][[奈良]][[大阪]][[愛知]]岐阜福井石川[[新潟]]佐渡[[秋田]]神奈川[[東京]]や全国各地に存在します。

revision.text:
'''天王神社'''（てんのうじんじゃ）は、[[牛頭天王]]を祀る[[神社]]で、日本各地にある。明治の[[神仏分離]]の際、祭神を牛頭天王と習合されていた[[スサノオ]]としたり、古伝に記載された祭神に戻したりした神社もあり、その際に社名を変更した神社も数多い。しかし、現在でも牛頭天王を祭神とする神社が数社ある。 全国に天王の地名多少有るが天王神社や天王杉神社なども天王を祭神としている。
[[Category:祇園神社|*てんのうしんしや]] {{shinto-stub}}

revision.text:
'''天王神社'''（てんのうじんじゃ）は、[[牛頭天王]]を祀る[[神社]]で、日本各地にある。明治の[[神仏分離]]の際、祭神を牛頭天王と習合されていた[[スサノオ]]としたり、古伝に記載された祭神に戻したりした神社もあり、その際に社名を変更した神社も数多い。しかし、現在でも牛頭天王を祭神とするご利益の有る天王神社が奈良県京都府新潟県[[天王杉神社]]など全国に数社あり、地震雷天変地異から世界日本を御守護している。天孫降臨や神様が生まれるより古く世界日本の中心的の宇宙地球誕生古代の神々の神社であられる。
[[Category:祇園神社|*てんのうしんしや]] {{shinto-stub}} [[天王杉神社てんのうすぎじんじゃ]]

'''天王神社'''（てんのうじんじゃ）は、[[牛頭天王]]を祀る[[神社]]で、日本各地にある。明治の[[神仏分離]]の際、祭神を牛頭天王と習合されていた[[スサノオ]]としたり、古伝に記載された祭神に戻したりした神社もあり、その際に社名を変更した神社も数多い。しかし、現在でも牛頭天王を祭神とするご利益の有る天王神社が奈良県京都府新潟県[[天王杉神社]]など全国に数社あり、地震雷天変地異から世界日本を御守護している。天孫降臨や神様が生まれるより古く世界日本の中心的の宇宙地球誕生古代の神代の神社であられる。
[[Category:祇園神社|*てんのうしんしや]] {{shinto-stub}} [[天王杉神社てんのうすぎじんじゃ]]

'''天王神社'''（てんのうじんじゃ）は、[[牛頭天王]]を祀る[[神社]]で、日本各地にある。明治の[[神仏分離]]の際、祭神を牛頭天王と習合されていた[[スサノオ]]としたり、古伝に記載された祭神に戻したりした神社もあり、その際に社名を変更した神社も数多い。しかし、現在でも牛頭天王を祭神とするご利益の有る天王神社が奈良県京都府新潟県[[天王杉神社]]など全国に数社あり、地震雷天変地異から世界日本を御守護している。天孫降臨や神様が生まれるより古く世界日本の中心的の宇宙地球誕生古代の神代の神社であられる。（龍神とも関係あり）
[[Category:祇園神社|*てんのうしんしや]] {{shinto-stub}} [[天王杉神社てんのうすぎじんじゃ]]

'''天王神社'''（てんのうじんじゃ）は、[[牛頭天王]]を祀る[[神社]]で、日本各地にある。明治の[[神仏分離]]の際、祭神を牛頭天王と習合されていた[[スサノオ]]としたり、古伝に記載された祭神に戻したりした神社もあり、その際に社名を変更した神社も数多い。しかし、現在でも牛頭天王を祭神とするご利益の有る天王神社が奈良県京都府新潟県[[天王杉神社]]など全国に数社あり、地震雷天変地異から世界日本を御守護している。天孫降臨や神様が生まれるより古く世界日本の中心的の宇宙地球誕生古代の神代の神社であられる。（日本列島の守護神の龍神とも関係有る）
[[Category:祇園神社|*てんのうしんしや]] {{shinto-stub}} [[天王杉神社てんのうすぎじんじゃ]]

'''天王神社'''（てんのうじんじゃ）は、[[牛頭天王]]を祀る[[神社]]で、日本各地にある。明治の[[神仏分離]]の際、祭神を牛頭天王と習合されていた[[スサノオ]]としたり、古伝に記載された祭神に戻したりした神社もあり、その際に社名を変更した神社も数多い。しかし、現在でも牛頭天王を祭神とするご利益の有る天王神社が奈良県京都府新潟県[[天王杉神社]]など全国に数社あり、地震雷天変地異から世界日本を御守護している。天孫降臨や神様が生まれるより古く世界日本の中心的の宇宙地球誕生古代の神代の神社であられる。（日本列島の龍神形の龍神守護神とも関係有る）
[[Category:祇園神社|*てんのうしんしや]] {{shinto-stub}} [[天王杉神社てんのうすぎじんじゃ]]

revision.text:
'''天王神社'''（てんのうじんじゃ）は、旧来の[[牛頭天王]]を祀っていた[[神社]]である。
現在の祭神は、牛頭天王を祭神とする神社は数社のみで、主に[[神仏習合]]で牛頭天王と習合されていた[[スサノオ]]を祀っている神社が多く、[[明治]]の[[神仏分離]]の際、[[スサノオ]]以外に、古伝に記載された祭神に戻したりした神社もある。
明治以降、他社との[[合祀]]で社名を変更した神社も数多く、地域によってはその名残を天王という地名に見ることができる。
==関連項目== *[[祇園信仰]] *[[天王祭]] *[[天王洲]]
[[Category:祇園神社|*てんのうしんしや]] {{shinto-stub}}

revision.text:
'''天王神社'''（てんのうじんじゃ、てんおうじんじゃ）、'''天王社'''(てんのうしゃ、てんおうしゃ)は、旧来の[[牛頭天王]]を祀っていた[[神社]]である。
総本山は、[[愛知県]][[津島市]]にある[[津島神社]]。
現在の祭神は、牛頭天王を祭神とする神社は数社のみで、主に[[神仏習合]]で牛頭天王と習合されていた[[スサノオ]]を祀っている神社が多く、[[明治]]の[[神仏分離]]の際、[[スサノオ]]以外に、古伝に記載された祭神に戻したりした神社もある。
明治以降、他社との[[合祀]]で社名を変更した神社も数多く、地域によってはその名残を天王という地名に見ることができる。
==関連項目== *[[祇園信仰]] *[[天王祭]] *[[天王山]] *[[天王洲]] *[[津島神社]]－旧津島牛頭天王社

総本山は、[[愛知県]][[津島市]]にある[[津島神社]]。
現在の祭神は、牛頭天王を祭神とする神社は数社のみで、主に[[神仏習合]]で牛頭天王と習合されていた[[スサノオ]]を祀っている神社が多く、[[明治]]の[[神仏分離]]の際、[[スサノオ]]以外に、古伝に記載された祭神に戻したりした神社もある。

revision.text:
'''天王神社'''（てんのうじんじゃ、てんおうじんじゃ）、'''天王社'''(てんのうしゃ、てんおうしゃ)は、旧来の[[牛頭天王]]を祀っていた[[神社]]である。
総本山は、[[愛知県]][[津島市]]にある[[津島神社]]。
現在の祭神は、牛頭天王を祭神とする神社は数社のみで、主に[[神仏習合]]で牛頭天王と習合されていた[[スサノオ]]を祀っている神社が多く、[[明治]]の[[神仏分離]]の際、[[スサノオ]]以外に、古伝に記載された祭神に戻したりした神社もある。
明治以降、他社との[[合祀]]や、津島牛頭天王社の社名変更に伴い、[[津島神社]]などへの社名を変更した神社も数多い。 また、神仏分離と合祀の影響で、牛頭天王以外の祭神になっている神社や、牛頭天王をスサノオ以外の神として解釈しているところも散見できる。 地域によってはその名残を天王という地名に見ることができる。
==関連項目== *[[祇園信仰]] *[[天王祭]] *[[天王山]] *[[天王洲]] *[[津島神社]]－旧津島牛頭天王社 **[[津島信仰]]
[[Category:祇園神社|*てんのうしんしや]] {{shinto-stub}}

'''天王神社'''（てんのうじんじゃ、てんおうじんじゃ）（天王社より古代時代創設）、[[牛頭天王]]を祀っていた[[神社]]である。古代[[天照大神]]を助けた宇宙神信仰の[[天之御中主神]]の秩父神社と初代天王神社総本山は[[神武天皇]]稜近くの[[奈良]][[京都]][[秩父]]関東と江戸時代[[江戸]]東京より人口が多かった[[政令指定都市]]の[[新潟]]にもある。
'''天王社'''(てんのうしゃ、てんおうしゃ)は、旧来の[[牛頭天王]]を祀っていた[[神社]]である。天王社の総本山は、[[愛知県]][[津島市]]にある[[津島神社]]。
現在の祭神は、牛頭天王を祭神とする神社は数社のみで、主に[[神仏習合]]で牛頭天王と習合されていた[[スサノオ]]を祀っている神社が多く、[[明治]]の[[神仏分離]]の際、[[スサノオ]]以外に、古伝に記載された祭神に戻したりした神社もある。
明治以降、他社との[[合祀]]や、津島牛頭天王社の社名変更に伴い、[[津島神社]]などへの社名を変更した神社も数多い。 また、神仏分離と合祀の影響で、牛頭天王以外の祭神になっている神社や、牛頭天王をスサノオ以外の神として解釈しているところも散見できる。 地域によってはその名残を天王という地名に見ることができる。
==関連項目== *[[祇園信仰]] *[[天王祭]] *[[天王山]] *[[天王洲]] *[[津島神社]]－旧津島牛頭天王社 **[[津島信仰]]
[[Category:祇園神社|*てんのうしんしや]] {{shinto-stub}}

'''天王神社'''（てんのうじんじゃ、てんおうじんじゃ）、'''天王社'''(てんのうしゃ、てんおうしゃ)は、旧来の[[牛頭天王]]を祀っていた[[神社]]である。
総本山は、[[愛知県]][[津島市]]にある[[津島神社]]。
現在の祭神は、牛頭天王を祭神とする神社は数社のみで、主に[[神仏習合]]で牛頭天王と習合されていた[[スサノオ]]を祀っている神社が多く、[[明治]]の[[神仏分離]]の際、[[スサノオ]]以外に、古伝に記載された祭神に戻したりした神社もある。
明治以降、他社との[[合祀]]や、津島牛頭天王社の社名変更に伴い、[[津島神社]]などへの社名を変更した神社も数多い。 また、神仏分離と合祀の影響で、牛頭天王以外の祭神になっている神社や、牛頭天王をスサノオ以外の神として解釈しているところも散見できる。 地域によってはその名残を天王という地名に見ることができる。

'''天王神社'''（てんのうじんじゃ、てんおうじんじゃ）、'''天王社'''(てんのうしゃ、てんおうしゃ)は、旧来の[[牛頭天王]]を祀っていた[[神社]]である。
現在の祭神は、牛頭天王を祭神とする神社は数社のみで、主に[[神仏習合]]で牛頭天王と習合されていた[[スサノオ]]を祀っている神社が多く、[[明治]]の[[神仏分離]]の際、[[スサノオ]]以外に、古伝に記載された祭神に戻したりした神社もある。
明治以降、他社との[[合祀]]や、津島牛頭天王社の社名変更に伴い、[[津島神社]]などへの社名を変更した神社も数多い。 また、神仏分離と合祀の影響で、牛頭天王以外の祭神になっている神社や、牛頭天王をスサノオ以外の神として解釈しているところも散見できる。 地域によってはその名残を天王という地名に見ることができる。
==関連項目== *[[祇園信仰]] *[[天王祭]] *[[天王山]] *[[天王洲]] *[[津島神社]]－旧津島牛頭天王社 **[[津島信仰]]
[[Category:祇園神社|*てんのうしんしや]] {{shinto-stub}}

'''天王神社'''（てんのうじんじゃ、てんおうじんじゃ）、'''天王社'''(てんのうしゃ、てんおうしゃ)は、旧来の[[牛頭天王]]を祀っていた[[神社]]である。 神代古代より奈良県に天王神社と新潟県遺跡に初代の天王杉神社あり（[[祟り神]]）。
現在の祭神は、牛頭天王を祭神とする神社は数社のみで、主に[[神仏習合]]で牛頭天王と習合されていた[[スサノオ]]を祀っている神社が多く、[[明治]]の[[神仏分離]]の際、[[スサノオ]]以外に、古伝に記載された祭神に戻したりした神社もある。
明治以降、他社との[[合祀]]や、津島牛頭天王社の社名変更に伴い、[[津島神社]]などへの社名を変更した神社も数多い。 また、神仏分離と合祀の影響で、牛頭天王以外の祭神になっている神社や、牛頭天王をスサノオ以外の神として解釈しているところも散見できる。 地域によってはその名残を天王という地名に見ることができる。

'''天王神社'''（てんのうじんじゃ、てんおうじんじゃ）、'''天王社'''(てんのうしゃ、てんおうしゃ)は、[[牛頭天王]]・[[スサノオ]]を祭神とする[[祇園信仰]]の神社。特に[[津島神社]]（[[愛知県]][[津島市]]）系統の神社にこの名称が使われることがみられる。他に祇園信仰に基づく神社名称としては、[[八坂神社_(曖昧さ回避)|八坂神社]]（八阪神社・[[弥栄神社]]）、[[祇園神社]]、[[広峯神社_(曖昧さ回避)|広峯神社]]、[[八雲神社]]、[[須賀神社]]、[[素盞嗚神社]]があり、時代や資料によって通用される。
現在の祭神は、牛頭天王を祭神とする神社は数社のみで、主に[[神仏習合]]で牛頭天王と習合されていた[[スサノオ]]を祀っている神社が多く、[[明治]]の[[神仏分離]]の際、[[スサノオ]]以外に、古伝に記載された祭神に戻したりした神社もある。
明治以降、他社との[[合祀]]や、津島牛頭天王社の社名変更に伴い、[[津島神社]]などへの社名を変更した神社も数多い。 また、神仏分離と合祀の影響で、牛頭天王以外の祭神になっている神社や、牛頭天王をスサノオ以外の神として解釈しているところも散見できる。 地域によってはその名残を天王という地名に見ることができる。

revision.text:
__NOTOC__ '''天王神社'''（てんのうじんじゃ、てんおうじんじゃ)、'''天王社'''（てんのうしゃ、てんおうしゃ）は、[[牛頭天王]]・[[スサノオ]]を祭神とする[[祇園信仰]]の神社。
[[津島神社]]（愛知県津島市）系統の神社にこの名称が使われることが見られる。他に祇園信仰に基づく神社名称としては、[[八坂神社 (曖昧さ回避)|八坂神社]]（八阪神社・[[弥栄神社]])、[[祇園神社]]、[[広峯神社 (曖昧さ回避)|広峯神社]]、[[八雲神社]]、[[須賀神社]]、[[素盞嗚神社]]があり、時代や資料によって通用される。
現在の祭神は、牛頭天王を祭神とする神社は数社のみで、主に[[神仏習合]]で牛頭天王と習合されていた[[スサノオ]]を祀っている神社が多く、明治の[[神仏分離]]の際、[[スサノオ]]以外に、古伝に記載された祭神に戻した神社もある。
明治以降、他社との[[合祀]]や、津島牛頭天王社の社名変更に伴い、津島神社などへの社名を変更した神社も数多い。また、神仏分離と合祀の影響で、牛頭天王以外の祭神になっている神社や、牛頭天王をスサノオ以外の神として解釈しているところも散見できる。地域によっては、その名残を天王という地名に見ることができる。
== 主な天王神社 == * [[天王神社 (七戸町)|天王神社]] – [[青森県]][[上北郡]][[七戸町]]七戸 * [[牛頭天王社 (葛飾区)|牛頭天王社]] – [[東京都]][[葛飾区]][[奥戸]] * [[天王神社 (千葉市)|天王神社]] – [[千葉県]][[千葉市]][[花見川区]]横戸町 * [[天王神社 (島田市)|天王神社]] – [[静岡県]][[島田市]]阪本 * [[天王神社 (西伊豆町)|天王神社]] – 静岡県[[賀茂郡]][[西伊豆町]]仁科 * [[天王神社 (新発田市)|天王神社]] – [[新潟県]][[新発田市]]天王 ： [[須佐之男神社 (新発田市)|須佐之男神社]]と背中合わせ * [[天王社八幡宮]] – [[京都府]][[京都市]][[右京区]]常盤東ノ町 * [[天王神社 (亀岡市)|天王神社]] – 京都府[[亀岡市]][[宮前町 (亀岡市)|宮前町]]神前 * [[天王神社 (木津川市)|天王神社]] – 京都府[[木津川市]]木津清水 * [[天王神社 (精華町)|天王神社]] – 京都府[[相楽郡]][[精華町]]菅井 * 天王神社 – [[兵庫県]][[明石市]]魚住町 ： [[素盞嗚神社 (明石市)|素盞嗚神社]]の通称 * [[天王神社 (橋本市御幸辻)|天王神社]] – [[和歌山県]][[橋本市]]御幸辻 * [[天王神社 (橋本市矢倉脇)|天王神社]] – 和歌山県橋本市矢倉脇 * [[天王八幡神社]] – [[岡山県]][[新見市]][[哲多町]] * [[天王社 (小松島市)|天王社]] – [[徳島県]][[小松島市]][[田野町 (小松島市)|田野町]]
== 関連項目 == * [[祇園信仰]] * [[天王祭]] * [[天王山]] * [[天王洲]] * [[津島神社]] * [[津島信仰]] * [[岡崎神社]] * [[朱智神社]] {{脚注ヘルプ}}

revision.text: __NOTOC__'''Tenno Jinja''' (天王神社、天王社) is a Shinto shrine dedicated to {{ill|Gozu Tennō|ja|牛頭天王}} and {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}}, central deities in {{ill|Gion Faith|ja|祇園信仰}}. This name is especially used for shrines in the lineage of {{ill|Tsushima Shrine|ja|津島神社}} in {{ill|Tsushima City, Aichi Prefecture|ja|津島市}}. Other shrines based on Gion Faith include {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|八坂神社 (曖昧さ回避)}}, {{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園神社}}, {{ill|Hiromine Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|広峯神社 (曖昧さ回避)}}, {{ill|Yakumo Shrine|ja|八雲神社}}, {{ill|Suga Shrine|ja|須賀神社}}, and {{ill|Susanoo Shrine|ja|素盞嗚神社}}, whose usage varies by era and source.Currently, only a few shrines still worship Gozu Tennō directly. Most shrines now enshrine {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}}, who was syncretized with Gozu Tennō under {{ill|Shinbutsu-shūgō|ja|神仏習合}}. During the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}} (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) in the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}}, some shrines reverted to deities listed in ancient records other than Susanoo.Since the Meiji period, many shrines have been merged with others or renamed to align with the Tsushima Gozu Tennōsha, such as renaming to {{ill|Tsushima Shrine|ja|津島神社}}. As a result of Shinbutsu bunri and shrine mergers, some shrines now enshrine deities other than Gozu Tennō or interpret Gozu Tennō as a deity distinct from Susanoo. In some regions, the influence of these shrines can still be seen in place names incorporating "Tenno."== List ==* {{ill|Tenno Shrine (Shichinohe)|ja|天王神社 (七戸町)}} - Aomori Prefecture, Kamikita District, Shichinohe, {{ill|Shichinohe|ja|七戸 (七戸町)}}* {{ill|Susanoo Shrine (Shibata)|ja|須佐之男神社 (新発田市)}} (Tenno Shrine) - Niigata Prefecture, Shibata, {{ill|Tenno (Shibata)|ja|天王 (新発田市)}}<ref>[http://www.niigata-kankou.or.jp/sys/data?page-id=10163], [http://www.niigata-u.com/files/ngt2009g/091010b1.html], [http://shibata-info.jp/archives/eventcal/%E5%A4%A9%E7%8E%8B%E3%81%BE%E3%81%A4%E3%82%8A]</ref>* {{ill|Tenno Shrine (Kameoka)|ja|天王神社 (亀岡市)}} - Kyoto Prefecture, Kameoka, {{ill|Miyamae Town|ja|宮前町 (亀岡市)}}, Kamisaki, Tenno Shita<ref>[http://agua.jpn.org/film/f99.html], [http://901-1000.wakkan.jp/927.html]</ref>* {{ill|Tenno Shrine Hachiman Shrine|ja|天王社八幡宮}}* {{ill|Tenno Shrine (Kizugawa)|ja|天王神社 (木津川市)}} - Kyoto Prefecture, Kizugawa, {{ill|Kizu Shimizu|ja|木津清水}}* {{ill|Tenno Shrine (Komatsushima)|ja|天王社 (小松島市)}} - Tokushima Prefecture, Komatsushima, {{ill|Tano Town (Komatsushima)|ja|田野町 (小松島市)}}* {{ill|Gozu Tenno Shrine|ja|牛頭天王神社}}* {{ill|Tenno Hachiman Shrine|ja|天王八幡神社}}* {{ill|Kenoda Suga Shrine|ja|健田須賀神社}}* {{ill|Tennosha Shrine (Komatsushima City)|ja|天王社 (小松島市)}}== See Also ==* {{ill|Gion Faith|ja|祇園信仰}}* {{ill|Gion and Tenno Festivals|ja|天王祭}}* ]][[:en:Mount_Tennōzan|Mount Tennōzan]]* {{ill|Tennōzu Isle|ja|天王洲}}* {{ill|Tsushima Shrine|ja|津島神社}} - Formerly Tsushima Gozu Tennōsha** {{ill|Tsushima Faith|ja|津島信仰}}* {{ill|Okazaki Shrine|ja|岡崎神社}}* {{ill|Shuchi Shrine|ja|朱智神社}}== References =={{Reflist}}{{Shinto Horizontal}}]][[Category:Gion Shrines]]]][[Category:Shinto stubs]]]][[Category:Buddhism stubs]]]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]]]][[Category:Tenno Shrine]]]][[Category:Shrines with identical names]]]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]]]][[Category:Tenno Shrine]]]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Tenno Shrine]]]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]]]][[Category:Tenno Shrine]]<!--note do not remove this category during translation-->]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]]]][[Category:Tenno Shrine]]]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Tenno Shrine]]]][[en:draft:Tenno Shrine]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Tenno Shrine}}]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]]]][[Category:Tenno Shrine]]]][[Category:dd]]]][[Category:Tenno Shrine]]]][[Category:dd]]]][[Category:mass bump]]]][[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]]]][[Category:Mass editing to refresh the first new Wikipedia redirects]]]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]]]][[Category:bump 1-26]]]][[Category:properly attributed pages]]]][[Category:Tenno Shrine]]]][[Category:properly attributed pages]]]][[ja:天王神社]]

revision.text:
__NOTOC__ '''天王神社'''（てんのうじんじゃ、てんおうじんじゃ)、'''天王社'''（てんのうしゃ、てんおうしゃ）は、[[牛頭天王]]・[[スサノオ]]を祭神とする[[祇園信仰]]の[[神社]]。
[[津島神社]]（愛知県津島市）系統の神社にこの名称が使われることが見られる。他に祇園信仰に基づく神社名称としては、[[八坂神社 (曖昧さ回避)|八坂神社]]（八阪神社・[[弥栄神社]])、[[祇園神社]]、[[広峯神社 (曖昧さ回避)|広峯神社]]、[[八雲神社]]、[[須賀神社]]、[[素盞嗚神社]]があり、時代や資料によって通用される。
現在の祭神は、牛頭天王を祭神とする神社は数社のみで、主に[[神仏習合]]で牛頭天王と習合されていた[[スサノオ]]を祀っている神社が多く、明治の[[神仏分離]]の際、[[スサノオ]]以外に、古伝に記載された祭神に戻した神社もある。
明治以降、他社との[[合祀]]や、津島牛頭天王社の社名変更に伴い、津島神社などへの社名を変更した神社も数多い。また、神仏分離と合祀の影響で、牛頭天王以外の祭神になっている神社や、牛頭天王をスサノオ以外の神として解釈しているところも散見できる。地域によっては、その名残を天王という地名に見ることができる。
== 主な天王神社 == * [[天王神社 (七戸町)|天王神社]] – [[青森県]][[上北郡]][[七戸町]]七戸 * [[天王神社 (千葉市)|天王神社]] – [[千葉県]][[千葉市]][[花見川区]]横戸町 * [[天王神社 (島田市)|天王神社]] – [[静岡県]][[島田市]]阪本 * [[天王神社 (西伊豆町)|天王神社]] – 静岡県[[賀茂郡]][[西伊豆町]]仁科 * [[天王神社 (新発田市)|天王神社]] – [[新潟県]][[新発田市]]天王 ： [[須佐之男神社 (新発田市)|須佐之男神社]]と背中合わせ * [[天王神社 (亀岡市)|天王神社]] – [[京都府]][[亀岡市]][[宮前町 (亀岡市)|宮前町]]神前 * [[天王神社 (木津川市)|天王神社]] – 京都府[[木津川市]]木津清水 * [[天王神社 (精華町)|天王神社]] – 京都府[[相楽郡]][[精華町]]菅井 * 天王神社 – [[兵庫県]][[明石市]]魚住町 ： [[素盞嗚神社 (明石市)|素盞嗚神社]]の通称 * [[天王神社 (橋本市御幸辻)|天王神社]] – [[和歌山県]][[橋本市]]御幸辻 * [[天王神社 (橋本市矢倉脇)|天王神社]] – 和歌山県橋本市矢倉脇 * [[天王八幡神社]] – [[岡山県]][[新見市]][[哲多町]]
== 主な天王社 == * [[牛頭天王社 (葛飾区)|牛頭天王社]] – [[東京都]][[葛飾区]][[奥戸]] * 天王社 – [[東京都]][[品川区]][[北品川]] ： [[荏原神社]] * [[天王社 (横浜市緑区)|天王社]] – [[神奈川県]][[横浜市]][[緑区 (横浜市)|緑区]][[長津田]]町 * [[天王社 (豊川市)|天王社]] – [[愛知県]][[豊川市]][[千歳通 (豊川市)|千歳通]] * [[天王社八幡宮]] – 京都府[[京都市]][[右京区]]常盤東ノ町 * [[天王社 (小松島市)|天王社]] – [[徳島県]][[小松島市]][[田野町 (小松島市)|田野町]]
== 関連項目 == * [[祇園信仰]] * [[津島神社]] * [[津島信仰]] * [[天王祭]] * [[天王山]] * [[天王洲]] {{脚注ヘルプ}}
{{神道 横}} [[Category:祇園神社|!てんのうしんしや]] {{DEFAULTSORT:てんのうしんしや}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Buddhism-stub}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:天王神社]]
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11443028]]
Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:54, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
__NOTOC__
'''Tenno Jinja''' (天王神社、天王社) is a Shinto shrine dedicated to [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō]] and [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], central deities in [[:en:Gion cult|Gion Faith]]. This name is especially used for shrines in the lineage of [[:en:Tsushima Shrine|Tsushima Shrine]] in [[:en:Tsushima, Aichi|Tsushima City, Aichi Prefecture]]. Other shrines based on Gion Faith include {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|八坂神社 (曖昧さ回避)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka Shrine (disambiguation)|qq=}}, {{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Shrine|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Gion shrine|WD=Q11588442}}, {{ill|Hiromine Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|広峯神社 (曖昧さ回避)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiromine Shrine (disambiguation)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Yakumo Shrine|ja|八雲神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakumo Shrine|qq=|1=Yakumo shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391847}}, {{ill|Suga Shrine|ja|須賀神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suga Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Susanoo Shrine|ja|素盞嗚神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo Shrine|qq=}}, whose usage varies by era and source.

Currently, only a few shrines still worship Gozu Tennō directly. Most shrines now enshrine [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], who was syncretized with Gozu Tennō under [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]]. During the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) in the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], some shrines reverted to deities listed in ancient records other than Susanoo.

Since the Meiji period, many shrines have been merged with others or renamed to align with the Tsushima Gozu Tennōsha, such as renaming to [[:en:Tsushima Shrine|Tsushima Shrine]]. As a result of Shinbutsu bunri and shrine mergers, some shrines now enshrine deities other than Gozu Tennō or interpret Gozu Tennō as a deity distinct from Susanoo. In some regions, the influence of these shrines can still be seen in place names incorporating "Tenno."

==Merged second translation==
__NOTOC__
'''Tennō Shrine''' (天王神社) and '''Tennō-sha''' (天王社) are Shinto shrines of the [[:en:Gion cult|Gion Faith]] tradition that enshrine [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō]] and [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]].

It is observed that this name is used in shrines belonging to the lineage of [[:en:Tsushima Shrine|Tsushima Shrine]] (Tsushima City, Aichi Prefecture). Other shrine names based on the Gion faith include {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|八坂神社 (曖昧さ回避)|lt=Yasaka Shrine|lt_ja=八坂神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka Shrine (disambiguation)|qq=}} (also known as {{ill|Yaei Shrine|ja|弥栄神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yaei Shrine|qq=|1=Yaei Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487447}}), {{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Shrine|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Gion shrine|WD=Q11588442}}, {{ill|Hiromine Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|広峯神社 (曖昧さ回避)|lt=Hiromine Shrine|lt_ja=広峯神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiromine Shrine (disambiguation)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Yakumo Shrine|ja|八雲神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakumo Shrine|qq=|1=Yakumo shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391847}}, {{ill|Suga Shrine|ja|須賀神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suga Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Susanoo Shrine|ja|素盞嗚神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo Shrine|qq=}}, names which vary according to the era and source.

Among the current enshrined deities, only a few shrines have [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō]] as their primary deity, while many shrines enshrine [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], who had been syncretized with Gozu Tennō through [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu Shūgō]]. During the Meiji era's separation of Shinto and Buddhism ([[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu Bunri]]), some shrines reverted to the deities recorded in ancient tradition rather than enshrining Susanoo.

Since the Meiji era, due to joint enshrinement ({{ill|Joint Enshrinement|ja|合祀|1=co-Enshrinement|2=ja|3=合祀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Joint Enshrinement|13=User:Immanuelle/co-Enshrinement|qq=}}) with other shrines and the renaming of institutions such as the Tsushima Gozu Tennō Shrine, many shrines have changed their names to, for example, Tsushima Shrine. Additionally, influenced by the separation of Shinto and Buddhism and such joint enshrinement, there are shrines where the enshrined deity is not [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō]], and some where Gozu Tennō is interpreted as a deity other than Susanoo. In certain regions, the legacy of this phenomenon can even be seen in local place names bearing "Tennō."

Currently, only a few shrines still worship Gozu Tennō directly. During the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) in the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], some shrines reverted to deities listed in ancient records other than Susanoo.

Since the Meiji period, many shrines have been merged with others or renamed to align with the Tsushima Gozu Tennōsha, such as renaming to [[:en:Tsushima Shrine|Tsushima Shrine]]. ==Merged second translation==
__NOTOC__
'''Tennō Shrine''' (天王神社) and '''Tennō-sha''' (天王社) are Shinto shrines of the [[:en:Gion cult|Gion Faith]] tradition that enshrine [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō]] and [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]].

It is observed that this name is used in shrines belonging to the lineage of [[:en:Tsushima Shrine|Tsushima Shrine]] (Tsushima City, Aichi Prefecture). Other shrine names based on the Gion faith include {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|八坂神社 (曖昧さ回避)|lt=Yasaka Shrine|lt_ja=八坂神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka Shrine (disambiguation)|qq=}} (also known as {{ill|Yaei Shrine|ja|弥栄神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yaei Shrine|qq=|1=Yaei Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487447}}), {{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Shrine|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Gion shrine|WD=Q11588442}}, {{ill|Hiromine Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|広峯神社 (曖昧さ回避)|lt=Hiromine Shrine|lt_ja=広峯神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiromine Shrine (disambiguation)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Yakumo Shrine|ja|八雲神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakumo Shrine|qq=|1=Yakumo shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391847}}, {{ill|Suga Shrine|ja|須賀神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suga Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Susanoo Shrine|ja|素盞嗚神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo Shrine|qq=}}, names which vary according to the era and source.

Among the current enshrined deities, only a few shrines have [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō]] as their primary deity, while many shrines enshrine [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], who had been syncretized with Gozu Tennō through [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu Shūgō]]. During the Meiji era's separation of Shinto and Buddhism ([[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu Bunri]]), some shrines reverted to the deities recorded in ancient tradition rather than enshrining Susanoo.

Since the Meiji era, due to joint enshrinement ({{ill|Joint Enshrinement|ja|合祀|1=co-Enshrinement|2=ja|3=合祀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Joint Enshrinement|13=User:Immanuelle/co-Enshrinement|qq=}}) with other shrines and the renaming of institutions such as the Tsushima Gozu Tennō Shrine, many shrines have changed their names to, for example, Tsushima Shrine. Additionally, influenced by the separation of Shinto and Buddhism and such joint enshrinement, there are shrines where the enshrined deity is not [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō]], and some where Gozu Tennō is interpreted as a deity other than Susanoo. In certain regions, the legacy of this phenomenon can even be seen in local place names bearing "Tennō."

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11443028]]
Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:54, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
__NOTOC__
'''Tenno Jinja''' (天王神社、天王社) is a Shinto shrine dedicated to [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō]] and [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], central deities in [[:en:Gion cult|Gion Faith]]. This name is especially used for shrines in the lineage of [[:en:Tsushima Shrine|Tsushima Shrine]] in [[:en:Tsushima, Aichi|Tsushima City, Aichi Prefecture]]. Other shrines based on Gion Faith include {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|八坂神社 (曖昧さ回避)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka Shrine (disambiguation)|qq=}}, {{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Gion shrine|WD=Q11588442}}, {{ill|Hiromine Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|広峯神社 (曖昧さ回避)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiromine Shrine (disambiguation)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Yakumo Shrine|ja|八雲神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakumo Shrine|1=Yakumo shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391847}}, {{ill|Suga Shrine|ja|須賀神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suga Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Susanoo Shrine|ja|素盞嗚神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo Shrine|qq=}}, whose usage varies by era and source.

Currently, only a few shrines still worship Gozu Tennō directly. Most shrines now enshrine [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], who was syncretized with Gozu Tennō under [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]]. During the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) in the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], some shrines reverted to deities listed in ancient records other than Susanoo.

Since the Meiji period, many shrines have been merged with others or renamed to align with the Tsushima Gozu Tennōsha, such as renaming to [[:en:Tsushima Shrine|Tsushima Shrine]]. As a result of Shinbutsu bunri and shrine mergers, some shrines now enshrine deities other than Gozu Tennō or interpret Gozu Tennō as a deity distinct from Susanoo. In some regions, the influence of these shrines can still be seen in place names incorporating "Tenno."

* {{ill|Tenno Shrine Hachiman Shrine|ja|天王社八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenno Shrine Hachiman Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

'''Tennō Shrine''' (天王神社) and '''Tennō-sha''' (天王社) are Shinto shrines of the [[:en:Gion cult|Gion Faith]] tradition that enshrine [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō]] and [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]].

It is observed that this name is used in shrines belonging to the lineage of [[:en:Tsushima Shrine|Tsushima Shrine]] (Tsushima City, Aichi Prefecture). Other shrine names based on the Gion faith include {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|八坂神社 (曖昧さ回避)|lt=Yasaka Shrine|lt_ja=八坂神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka Shrine (disambiguation)|qq=}} (also known as {{ill|Yaei Shrine|ja|弥栄神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yaei Shrine|1=Yaei Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487447}}), {{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Gion shrine|WD=Q11588442}}, {{ill|Hiromine Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|広峯神社 (曖昧さ回避)|lt=Hiromine Shrine|lt_ja=広峯神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiromine Shrine (disambiguation)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Yakumo Shrine|ja|八雲神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakumo Shrine|1=Yakumo shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391847}}, {{ill|Suga Shrine|ja|須賀神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suga Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Susanoo Shrine|ja|素盞嗚神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo Shrine|qq=}}, names which vary according to the era and source.

Among the current enshrined deities, only a few shrines have [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō]] as their primary deity, while many shrines enshrine [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], who had been syncretized with Gozu Tennō through [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu Shūgō]]. During the Meiji era's separation of Shinto and Buddhism ([[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu Bunri]]), some shrines reverted to the deities recorded in ancient tradition rather than enshrining Susanoo.

Since the Meiji era, due to joint enshrinement ({{ill|Joint Enshrinement|ja|合祀|1=co-Enshrinement|2=ja|3=合祀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Joint Enshrinement|13=User:Immanuelle/co-Enshrinement|qq=}}) with other shrines and the renaming of institutions such as the Tsushima Gozu Tennō Shrine, many shrines have changed their names to, for example, Tsushima Shrine. Additionally, influenced by the separation of Shinto and Buddhism and such joint enshrinement, there are shrines where the enshrined deity is not [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō]], and some where Gozu Tennō is interpreted as a deity other than Susanoo. In certain regions, the legacy of this phenomenon can even be seen in local place names bearing "Tennō."

Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:54, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
__NOTOC__
'''Tenno Jinja''' (天王神社、天王社) is a Shinto shrine dedicated to [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō]] and [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], central deities in [[:en:Gion cult|Gion Faith]]. This name is especially used for shrines in the lineage of [[:en:Tsushima Shrine|Tsushima Shrine]] in [[:en:Tsushima, Aichi|Tsushima City, Aichi Prefecture]]. Other shrines based on Gion Faith include {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|八坂神社 (曖昧さ回避)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka Shrine (disambiguation)|qq=}}, {{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Gion shrine|WD=Q11588442}}, {{ill|Hiromine Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|広峯神社 (曖昧さ回避)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiromine Shrine (disambiguation)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Yakumo Shrine|ja|八雲神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakumo Shrine|1=Yakumo shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391847}}, {{ill|Suga Shrine|ja|須賀神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suga Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Susanoo Shrine|ja|素盞嗚神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo Shrine|qq=}}, whose usage varies by era and source.

Currently, only a few shrines still worship Gozu Tennō directly. Most shrines now enshrine [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], who was syncretized with Gozu Tennō under [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]]. During the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) in the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], some shrines reverted to deities listed in ancient records other than Susanoo.

Since the Meiji period, many shrines have been merged with others or renamed to align with the Tsushima Gozu Tennōsha, such as renaming to [[:en:Tsushima Shrine|Tsushima Shrine]]. As a result of Shinbutsu bunri and shrine mergers, some shrines now enshrine deities other than Gozu Tennō or interpret Gozu Tennō as a deity distinct from Susanoo. In some regions, the influence of these shrines can still be seen in place names incorporating "Tenno."

* {{ill|Tenno Shrine Hachiman Shrine|ja|天王社八幡宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenno Shrine Hachiman Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Tenno Shrine (Kizugawa)|ja|天王神社 (木津川市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenno Shrine (Kizugawa)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Kyoto Prefecture, Kizugawa, {{ill|Kizu Shimizu|ja|木津清水|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kizu Shimizu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:天王神社]]
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11443028]]
Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:54, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
__NOTOC__
'''Tenno Jinja''' (天王神社、天王社) is a Shinto shrine dedicated to [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō]] and [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], central deities in [[:en:Gion cult|Gion Faith]]. This name is especially used for shrines in the lineage of [[:en:Tsushima Shrine|Tsushima Shrine]] in [[:en:Tsushima, Aichi|Tsushima City, Aichi Prefecture]]. Other shrines based on Gion Faith include {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|八坂神社 (曖昧さ回避)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka Shrine (disambiguation)|qq=}}, {{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Gion shrine|WD=Q11588442}}, {{ill|Hiromine Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|広峯神社 (曖昧さ回避)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiromine Shrine (disambiguation)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Yakumo Shrine|ja|八雲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakumo Shrine|1=Yakumo shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391847}}, {{ill|Suga Shrine|ja|須賀神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suga Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Susanoo Shrine|ja|素盞嗚神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo Shrine|qq=}}, whose usage varies by era and source.

Currently, only a few shrines still worship Gozu Tennō directly. Most shrines now enshrine [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], who was syncretized with Gozu Tennō under [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]]. During the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) in the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], some shrines reverted to deities listed in ancient records other than Susanoo.

Since the Meiji period, many shrines have been merged with others or renamed to align with the Tsushima Gozu Tennōsha, such as renaming to [[:en:Tsushima Shrine|Tsushima Shrine]]. As a result of Shinbutsu bunri and shrine mergers, some shrines now enshrine deities other than Gozu Tennō or interpret Gozu Tennō as a deity distinct from Susanoo. In some regions, the influence of these shrines can still be seen in place names incorporating "Tenno."

==Merged second translation==
__NOTOC__
'''Tennō Shrine''' (天王神社) and '''Tennō-sha''' (天王社) are Shinto shrines of the [[:en:Gion cult|Gion Faith]] tradition that enshrine [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō]] and [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]].

It is observed that this name is used in shrines belonging to the lineage of [[:en:Tsushima Shrine|Tsushima Shrine]] (Tsushima City, Aichi Prefecture). Other shrine names based on the Gion faith include {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|八坂神社 (曖昧さ回避)|lt=Yasaka Shrine|lt_ja=八坂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka Shrine (disambiguation)|qq=}} (also known as {{ill|Yaei Shrine|ja|弥栄神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yaei Shrine|1=Yaei Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487447}}), {{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Gion shrine|WD=Q11588442}}, {{ill|Hiromine Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|広峯神社 (曖昧さ回避)|lt=Hiromine Shrine|lt_ja=広峯神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiromine Shrine (disambiguation)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Yakumo Shrine|ja|八雲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakumo Shrine|1=Yakumo shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391847}}, {{ill|Suga Shrine|ja|須賀神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suga Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Susanoo Shrine|ja|素盞嗚神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo Shrine|qq=}}, names which vary according to the era and source.

Among the current enshrined deities, only a few shrines have [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō]] as their primary deity, while many shrines enshrine [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], who had been syncretized with Gozu Tennō through [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu Shūgō]]. During the Meiji era's separation of Shinto and Buddhism ([[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu Bunri]]), some shrines reverted to the deities recorded in ancient tradition rather than enshrining Susanoo.

Since the Meiji era, due to joint enshrinement ({{ill|Joint Enshrinement|ja|合祀|1=co-Enshrinement|2=ja|3=合祀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Joint Enshrinement|13=User:Immanuelle/co-Enshrinement|qq=}}) with other shrines and the renaming of institutions such as the Tsushima Gozu Tennō Shrine, many shrines have changed their names to, for example, Tsushima Shrine. Additionally, influenced by the separation of Shinto and Buddhism and such joint enshrinement, there are shrines where the enshrined deity is not [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō]], and some where Gozu Tennō is interpreted as a deity other than Susanoo. In certain regions, the legacy of this phenomenon can even be seen in local place names bearing "Tennō."

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11443028]]
Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:54, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
__NOTOC__
'''Tenno Jinja''' (天王神社、天王社) is a Shinto shrine dedicated to [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō]] and [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], central deities in [[:en:Gion cult|Gion Faith]]. This name is especially used for shrines in the lineage of [[:en:Tsushima Shrine|Tsushima Shrine]] in [[:en:Tsushima, Aichi|Tsushima City, Aichi Prefecture]]. Other shrines based on Gion Faith include {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|八坂神社 (曖昧さ回避)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka Shrine (disambiguation)|qq=}}, {{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Gion shrine|WD=Q11588442}}, {{ill|Hiromine Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|広峯神社 (曖昧さ回避)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiromine Shrine (disambiguation)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Yakumo Shrine|ja|八雲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakumo Shrine|1=Yakumo shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391847}}, {{ill|Suga Shrine|ja|須賀神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suga Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Susanoo Shrine|ja|素盞嗚神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo Shrine|qq=}}, whose usage varies by era and source.

Currently, only a few shrines still worship Gozu Tennō directly. Most shrines now enshrine [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], who was syncretized with Gozu Tennō under [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]]. During the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) in the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], some shrines reverted to deities listed in ancient records other than Susanoo.

Since the Meiji period, many shrines have been merged with others or renamed to align with the Tsushima Gozu Tennōsha, such as renaming to [[:en:Tsushima Shrine|Tsushima Shrine]]. As a result of Shinbutsu bunri and shrine mergers, some shrines now enshrine deities other than Gozu Tennō or interpret Gozu Tennō as a deity distinct from Susanoo. In some regions, the influence of these shrines can still be seen in place names incorporating "Tenno."

* {{ill|Susanoo Shrine (Shibata)|ja|須佐之男神社 (新発田市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo Shrine (Shibata)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Tenno Shrine) - Niigata Prefecture, Shibata, {{ill|Tenno (Shibata)|ja|天王 (新発田市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenno (Shibata)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref>[http://www.niigata-kankou.or.jp/sys/data?page-id=10163], [http://www.niigata-u.com/files/ngt2009g/091010b1.html], [http://shibata-info.jp/archives/eventcal/%E5%A4%A9%E7%8E%8B%E3%81%BE%E3%81%A4%E3%82%8A]</ref>
* {{ill|Tenno Shrine (Kameoka)|ja|天王神社 (亀岡市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenno Shrine (Kameoka)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Kyoto Prefecture, Kameoka, {{ill|Miyamae Town|ja|宮前町 (亀岡市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamae Town|1=Miyazakichou|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q86733848}}, Kamisaki, Tenno Shita<ref>[http://agua.jpn.org/film/f99.html], [http://901-1000.wakkan.jp/927.html]</ref>

* {{ill|Tenno Shrine (Kizugawa)|ja|天王神社 (木津川市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenno Shrine (Kizugawa)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Kyoto Prefecture, Kizugawa, {{ill|Kizu Shimizu|ja|木津清水|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kizu Shimizu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Tenno Shrine (Komatsushima)|ja|天王社 (小松島市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenno Shrine (Komatsushima)|qq=}} - Tokushima Prefecture, Komatsushima, {{ill|Tano Town (Komatsushima)|ja|田野町 (小松島市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tano Town (Komatsushima)|1=Tano, Komatsushima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577483}}

==Merged second translation==
__NOTOC__
'''Tennō Shrine''' (天王神社) and '''Tennō-sha''' (天王社) are Shinto shrines of the [[:en:Gion cult|Gion Faith]] tradition that enshrine [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō]] and [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]].

It is observed that this name is used in shrines belonging to the lineage of [[:en:Tsushima Shrine|Tsushima Shrine]] (Tsushima City, Aichi Prefecture). Other shrine names based on the Gion faith include {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|八坂神社 (曖昧さ回避)|lt=Yasaka Shrine|lt_ja=八坂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka Shrine (disambiguation)|qq=}} (also known as {{ill|Yaei Shrine|ja|弥栄神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yaei Shrine|1=Yaei Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487447}}), {{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Gion shrine|WD=Q11588442}}, {{ill|Hiromine Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|広峯神社 (曖昧さ回避)|lt=Hiromine Shrine|lt_ja=広峯神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiromine Shrine (disambiguation)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Yakumo Shrine|ja|八雲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakumo Shrine|1=Yakumo shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391847}}, {{ill|Suga Shrine|ja|須賀神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suga Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Susanoo Shrine|ja|素盞嗚神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo Shrine|qq=}}, names which vary according to the era and source.

Among the current enshrined deities, only a few shrines have [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō]] as their primary deity, while many shrines enshrine [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], who had been syncretized with Gozu Tennō through [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu Shūgō]]. During the Meiji era's separation of Shinto and Buddhism ([[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu Bunri]]), some shrines reverted to the deities recorded in ancient tradition rather than enshrining Susanoo.

Since the Meiji era, due to joint enshrinement ({{ill|Joint Enshrinement|ja|合祀|1=co-Enshrinement|2=ja|3=合祀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Joint Enshrinement|13=User:Immanuelle/co-Enshrinement|qq=}}) with other shrines and the renaming of institutions such as the Tsushima Gozu Tennō Shrine, many shrines have changed their names to, for example, Tsushima Shrine. Additionally, influenced by the separation of Shinto and Buddhism and such joint enshrinement, there are shrines where the enshrined deity is not [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō]], and some where Gozu Tennō is interpreted as a deity other than Susanoo. In certain regions, the legacy of this phenomenon can even be seen in local place names bearing "Tennō."

== Main Tennō Shrines ==

* {{ill|Tennō Shrine (Shimada)|ja|天王神社 (島田市)|lt=Tennō Shrine|lt_ja=天王神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tennō Shrine (Shimada)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]][[:en:Shimada, Shizuoka|Shimada City]] Sakamoto
* {{ill|Tennō Shrine (Nishiizu)|ja|天王神社 (西伊豆町)|lt=Tennō Shrine|lt_ja=天王神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tennō Shrine (Nishiizu)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]][[:en:Kamo District, Shizuoka|Kamo District]][[:en:Nishiizu|Nishiizu Town]] Ninashina
* {{ill|Tennō Shrine (Shin-Hatsuta)|ja|天王神社 (新発田市)|lt=Tennō Shrine|lt_ja=天王神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tennō Shrine (Shin-Hatsuta)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – [[:en:Niigata Prefecture|Niigata Prefecture]][[:en:Shibata, Niigata|Shin-Hatsuta City]] Tennō: in a back-to-back arrangement with {{ill|Susanoo Shrine (Shin-Hatsuta)|ja|須佐之男神社 (新発田市)|lt=Susanoo Shrine|lt_ja=須佐之男神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo Shrine (Shin-Hatsuta)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Tennōsha Hachiman Shrine|ja|天王社八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tennōsha Hachiman Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]][[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]][[:en:Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|Ukyō-ku, Kyoto]] Tokiwahigashinomachi
* {{ill|Tennō Shrine (Kameoka)|ja|天王神社 (亀岡市)|lt=Tennō Shrine|lt_ja=天王神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tennō Shrine (Kameoka)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]][[:en:Kameoka, Kyoto|Kameoka City]]{{ill|Miyamae Town (Kameoka)|ja|宮前町 (亀岡市)|lt=Miyamae Town|lt_ja=宮前町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamae Town (Kameoka)|1=Miyazakichou|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q86733848}} Kamizengen
* {{ill|Tennō Shrine (Kizugawa)|ja|天王神社 (木津川市)|lt=Tennō Shrine|lt_ja=天王神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tennō Shrine (Kizugawa)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]][[:en:Kizugawa, Kyoto|Kizugawa City]] Kizu Kiyomizu
* {{ill|Tennō Shrine (Seika-chō)|ja|天王神社 (精華町)|lt=Tennō Shrine|lt_ja=天王神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tennō Shrine (Seika-chō)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]][[:en:Sōraku District, Kyoto|Sōraku District]][[:en:Seika, Kyoto|Seika Town]] Sugai

==Merged second translation==
__NOTOC__
'''Tennō Shrine''' (天王神社) and '''Tennō-sha''' (天王社) are Shinto shrines of the [[:en:Gion cult|Gion Faith]] tradition that enshrine [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō]] and [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]].

It is observed that this name is used in shrines belonging to the lineage of [[:en:Tsushima Shrine|Tsushima Shrine]] (Tsushima City, Aichi Prefecture). Other shrine names based on the Gion faith include {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|八坂神社 (曖昧さ回避)|lt=Yasaka Shrine|lt_ja=八坂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka Shrine (disambiguation)|qq=}} (also known as {{ill|Yaei Shrine|ja|弥栄神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yaei Shrine|1=Yaei Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487447}}), {{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Gion shrine|WD=Q11588442}}, {{ill|Hiromine Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|広峯神社 (曖昧さ回避)|lt=Hiromine Shrine|lt_ja=広峯神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiromine Shrine (disambiguation)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Yakumo Shrine|ja|八雲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakumo Shrine|1=Yakumo shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391847}}, {{ill|Suga Shrine|ja|須賀神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suga Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Susanoo Shrine|ja|素盞嗚神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo Shrine|qq=}}, names which vary according to the era and source.

Among the current enshrined deities, only a few shrines have [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō]] as their primary deity, while many shrines enshrine [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], who had been syncretized with Gozu Tennō through [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu Shūgō]]. During the Meiji era's separation of Shinto and Buddhism ([[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu Bunri]]), some shrines reverted to the deities recorded in ancient tradition rather than enshrining Susanoo.

Since the Meiji era, due to joint enshrinement ({{ill|Joint Enshrinement|ja|合祀|1=co-Enshrinement|2=ja|3=合祀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Joint Enshrinement|13=User:Immanuelle/co-Enshrinement|qq=}}) with other shrines and the renaming of institutions such as the Tsushima Gozu Tennō Shrine, many shrines have changed their names to, for example, Tsushima Shrine. Additionally, influenced by the separation of Shinto and Buddhism and such joint enshrinement, there are shrines where the enshrined deity is not [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō]], and some where Gozu Tennō is interpreted as a deity other than Susanoo. In certain regions, the legacy of this phenomenon can even be seen in local place names bearing "Tennō."

* {{ill|Tennō Shrine (Shin-Hatsuta)|ja|天王神社 (新発田市)|lt=Tennō Shrine|lt_ja=天王神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tennō Shrine (Shin-Hatsuta)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – [[:en:Niigata Prefecture|Niigata Prefecture]][[:en:Shibata, Niigata|Shin-Hatsuta City]] Tennō: in a back-to-back arrangement with {{ill|Susanoo Shrine (Shin-Hatsuta)|ja|須佐之男神社 (新発田市)|lt=Susanoo Shrine|lt_ja=須佐之男神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo Shrine (Shin-Hatsuta)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:天王神社]]
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11443028]]
Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:54, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
__NOTOC__
'''Tenno Jinja''' (天王神社、天王社) is a Shinto shrine dedicated to [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō]] and [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], central deities in [[:en:Gion cult|Gion Faith]]. This name is especially used for shrines in the lineage of [[:en:Tsushima Shrine|Tsushima Shrine]] in [[:en:Tsushima, Aichi|Tsushima City, Aichi Prefecture]]. Other shrines based on Gion Faith include {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|八坂神社 (曖昧さ回避)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka Shrine (disambiguation)|qq=}}, {{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Gion shrine|WD=Q11588442}}, {{ill|Hiromine Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|広峯神社 (曖昧さ回避)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiromine Shrine (disambiguation)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Yakumo Shrine|ja|八雲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakumo Shrine|1=Yakumo shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391847}}, {{ill|Suga Shrine|ja|須賀神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suga Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Susanoo Shrine|ja|素盞嗚神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo Shrine|qq=}}, whose usage varies by era and source.

Currently, only a few shrines still worship Gozu Tennō directly. Most shrines now enshrine [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], who was syncretized with Gozu Tennō under [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]]. During the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) in the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], some shrines reverted to deities listed in ancient records other than Susanoo.

Since the Meiji period, many shrines have been merged with others or renamed to align with the Tsushima Gozu Tennōsha, such as renaming to [[:en:Tsushima Shrine|Tsushima Shrine]]. As a result of Shinbutsu bunri and shrine mergers, some shrines now enshrine deities other than Gozu Tennō or interpret Gozu Tennō as a deity distinct from Susanoo. In some regions, the influence of these shrines can still be seen in place names incorporating "Tenno."

* {{ill|Tenno Shrine (Shichinohe)|ja|天王神社 (七戸町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenno Shrine (Shichinohe)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Aomori Prefecture, Kamikita District, Shichinohe, {{ill|Shichinohe|ja|七戸 (七戸町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichinohe|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Susanoo Shrine (Shibata)|ja|須佐之男神社 (新発田市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo Shrine (Shibata)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Tenno Shrine) - Niigata Prefecture, Shibata, {{ill|Tenno (Shibata)|ja|天王 (新発田市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenno (Shibata)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref>[http://www.niigata-kankou.or.jp/sys/data?page-id=10163], [http://www.niigata-u.com/files/ngt2009g/091010b1.html], [http://shibata-info.jp/archives/eventcal/%E5%A4%A9%E7%8E%8B%E3%81%BE%E3%81%A4%E3%82%8A]</ref>
* {{ill|Tenno Shrine (Kameoka)|ja|天王神社 (亀岡市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenno Shrine (Kameoka)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Kyoto Prefecture, Kameoka, {{ill|Miyamae Town|ja|宮前町 (亀岡市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamae Town|1=Miyazakichou|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q86733848}}, Kamisaki, Tenno Shita<ref>[http://agua.jpn.org/film/f99.html], [http://901-1000.wakkan.jp/927.html]</ref>
* {{ill|Tenno Shrine Hachiman Shrine|ja|天王社八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenno Shrine Hachiman Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Tenno Shrine (Kizugawa)|ja|天王神社 (木津川市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenno Shrine (Kizugawa)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Kyoto Prefecture, Kizugawa, {{ill|Kizu Shimizu|ja|木津清水|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kizu Shimizu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Tenno Shrine (Komatsushima)|ja|天王社 (小松島市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenno Shrine (Komatsushima)|qq=}} - Tokushima Prefecture, Komatsushima, {{ill|Tano Town (Komatsushima)|ja|田野町 (小松島市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tano Town (Komatsushima)|1=Tano, Komatsushima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577483}}
* [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō神社]]
* {{ill|Tenno Hachiman Shrine|ja|天王八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenno Hachiman Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Kenoda Suga Shrine|ja|健田須賀神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenoda Suga Shrine|1=Takedasuga Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11387225}}

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11443028]]
Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:54, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
__NOTOC__
'''Tenno Jinja''' (天王神社、天王社) is a Shinto shrine dedicated to [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō]] and [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], central deities in [[:en:Gion cult|Gion Faith]]. This name is especially used for shrines in the lineage of [[:en:Tsushima Shrine|Tsushima Shrine]] in [[:en:Tsushima, Aichi|Tsushima City, Aichi Prefecture]]. Other shrines based on Gion Faith include {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|八坂神社 (曖昧さ回避)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka Shrine (disambiguation)|qq=}}, {{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Gion shrine|WD=Q11588442}}, {{ill|Hiromine Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|広峯神社 (曖昧さ回避)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiromine Shrine (disambiguation)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Yakumo Shrine|ja|八雲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakumo Shrine|1=Yakumo shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391847}}, {{ill|Suga Shrine|ja|須賀神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suga Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Susanoo Shrine|ja|素盞嗚神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo Shrine|qq=}}, whose usage varies by era and source.

Currently, only a few shrines still worship Gozu Tennō directly. Most shrines now enshrine [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], who was syncretized with Gozu Tennō under [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]]. During the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) in the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], some shrines reverted to deities listed in ancient records other than Susanoo.

Since the Meiji period, many shrines have been merged with others or renamed to align with the Tsushima Gozu Tennōsha, such as renaming to [[:en:Tsushima Shrine|Tsushima Shrine]]. As a result of Shinbutsu bunri and shrine mergers, some shrines now enshrine deities other than Gozu Tennō or interpret Gozu Tennō as a deity distinct from Susanoo. In some regions, the influence of these shrines can still be seen in place names incorporating "Tenno."

==Merged second translation==
__NOTOC__
'''Tennō Shrine''' (天王神社) and '''Tennō-sha''' (天王社) are Shinto shrines of the [[:en:Gion cult|Gion Faith]] tradition that enshrine [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō]] and [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]].

It is observed that this name is used in shrines belonging to the lineage of [[:en:Tsushima Shrine|Tsushima Shrine]] (Tsushima City, Aichi Prefecture). Other shrine names based on the Gion faith include {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|八坂神社 (曖昧さ回避)|lt=Yasaka Shrine|lt_ja=八坂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka Shrine (disambiguation)|qq=}} (also known as {{ill|Yaei Shrine|ja|弥栄神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yaei Shrine|1=Yaei Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487447}}), {{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Gion shrine|WD=Q11588442}}, {{ill|Hiromine Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|広峯神社 (曖昧さ回避)|lt=Hiromine Shrine|lt_ja=広峯神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiromine Shrine (disambiguation)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Yakumo Shrine|ja|八雲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakumo Shrine|1=Yakumo shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391847}}, {{ill|Suga Shrine|ja|須賀神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suga Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Susanoo Shrine|ja|素盞嗚神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo Shrine|qq=}}, names which vary according to the era and source.

Among the current enshrined deities, only a few shrines have [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō]] as their primary deity, while many shrines enshrine [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], who had been syncretized with Gozu Tennō through [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu Shūgō]]. During the Meiji era's separation of Shinto and Buddhism ([[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu Bunri]]), some shrines reverted to the deities recorded in ancient tradition rather than enshrining Susanoo.

Since the Meiji era, due to joint enshrinement ({{ill|Joint Enshrinement|ja|合祀|1=co-Enshrinement|2=ja|3=合祀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Joint Enshrinement|13=User:Immanuelle/co-Enshrinement|qq=}}) with other shrines and the renaming of institutions such as the Tsushima Gozu Tennō Shrine, many shrines have changed their names to, for example, Tsushima Shrine. Additionally, influenced by the separation of Shinto and Buddhism and such joint enshrinement, there are shrines where the enshrined deity is not [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō]], and some where Gozu Tennō is interpreted as a deity other than Susanoo. In certain regions, the legacy of this phenomenon can even be seen in local place names bearing "Tennō."

Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:54, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
__NOTOC__
'''Tenno Jinja''' (天王神社、天王社) is a Shinto shrine dedicated to [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō]] and [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], central deities in [[:en:Gion cult|Gion Faith]]. This name is especially used for shrines in the lineage of [[:en:Tsushima Shrine|Tsushima Shrine]] in [[:en:Tsushima, Aichi|Tsushima City, Aichi Prefecture]]. Other shrines based on Gion Faith include {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|八坂神社 (曖昧さ回避)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka Shrine (disambiguation)|qq=}}, {{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Gion shrine|WD=Q11588442}}, {{ill|Hiromine Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|広峯神社 (曖昧さ回避)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiromine Shrine (disambiguation)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Yakumo Shrine|ja|八雲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakumo Shrine|1=Yakumo shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391847}}, {{ill|Suga Shrine|ja|須賀神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suga Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Susanoo Shrine|ja|素盞嗚神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo Shrine|qq=}}, whose usage varies by era and source.

Currently, only a few shrines still worship Gozu Tennō directly. Most shrines now enshrine [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], who was syncretized with Gozu Tennō under [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]]. During the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) in the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], some shrines reverted to deities listed in ancient records other than Susanoo.

Since the Meiji period, many shrines have been merged with others or renamed to align with the Tsushima Gozu Tennōsha, such as renaming to [[:en:Tsushima Shrine|Tsushima Shrine]]. As a result of Shinbutsu bunri and shrine mergers, some shrines now enshrine deities other than Gozu Tennō or interpret Gozu Tennō as a deity distinct from Susanoo. In some regions, the influence of these shrines can still be seen in place names incorporating "Tenno."

==Merged second translation==
__NOTOC__
'''Tennō Shrine''' (天王神社) and '''Tennō-sha''' (天王社) are Shinto shrines of the [[:en:Gion cult|Gion Faith]] tradition that enshrine [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō]] and [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]].

It is observed that this name is used in shrines belonging to the lineage of [[:en:Tsushima Shrine|Tsushima Shrine]] (Tsushima City, Aichi Prefecture). Other shrine names based on the Gion faith include {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|八坂神社 (曖昧さ回避)|lt=Yasaka Shrine|lt_ja=八坂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka Shrine (disambiguation)|qq=}} (also known as {{ill|Yaei Shrine|ja|弥栄神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yaei Shrine|1=Yaei Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487447}}), {{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Gion shrine|WD=Q11588442}}, {{ill|Hiromine Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|広峯神社 (曖昧さ回避)|lt=Hiromine Shrine|lt_ja=広峯神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiromine Shrine (disambiguation)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Yakumo Shrine|ja|八雲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakumo Shrine|1=Yakumo shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391847}}, {{ill|Suga Shrine|ja|須賀神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suga Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Susanoo Shrine|ja|素盞嗚神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo Shrine|qq=}}, names which vary according to the era and source.

Among the current enshrined deities, only a few shrines have [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō]] as their primary deity, while many shrines enshrine [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], who had been syncretized with Gozu Tennō through [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu Shūgō]]. During the Meiji era's separation of Shinto and Buddhism ([[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu Bunri]]), some shrines reverted to the deities recorded in ancient tradition rather than enshrining Susanoo.

Since the Meiji era, due to joint enshrinement ({{ill|Joint Enshrinement|ja|合祀|1=co-Enshrinement|2=ja|3=合祀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Joint Enshrinement|13=User:Immanuelle/co-Enshrinement|qq=}}) with other shrines and the renaming of institutions such as the Tsushima Gozu Tennō Shrine, many shrines have changed their names to, for example, Tsushima Shrine. Additionally, influenced by the separation of Shinto and Buddhism and such joint enshrinement, there are shrines where the enshrined deity is not [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō]], and some where Gozu Tennō is interpreted as a deity other than Susanoo. In certain regions, the legacy of this phenomenon can even be seen in local place names bearing "Tennō."

== Main Tennō Shrines ==

* {{ill|Tennō Shrine (Shimada)|ja|天王神社 (島田市)|lt=Tennō Shrine|lt_ja=天王神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tennō Shrine (Shimada)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]][[:en:Shimada, Shizuoka|Shimada City]] Sakamoto
* {{ill|Tennō Shrine (Nishiizu)|ja|天王神社 (西伊豆町)|lt=Tennō Shrine|lt_ja=天王神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tennō Shrine (Nishiizu)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]][[:en:Kamo District, Shizuoka|Kamo District]][[:en:Nishiizu|Nishiizu Town]] Ninashina
* {{ill|Tennō Shrine (Shin-Hatsuta)|ja|天王神社 (新発田市)|lt=Tennō Shrine|lt_ja=天王神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tennō Shrine (Shin-Hatsuta)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – [[:en:Niigata Prefecture|Niigata Prefecture]][[:en:Shibata, Niigata|Shin-Hatsuta City]] Tennō: in a back-to-back arrangement with {{ill|Susanoo Shrine (Shin-Hatsuta)|ja|須佐之男神社 (新発田市)|lt=Susanoo Shrine|lt_ja=須佐之男神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo Shrine (Shin-Hatsuta)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Tennōsha Hachiman Shrine|ja|天王社八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tennōsha Hachiman Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]][[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]][[:en:Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|Ukyō-ku, Kyoto]] Tokiwahigashinomachi
* {{ill|Tennō Shrine (Kameoka)|ja|天王神社 (亀岡市)|lt=Tennō Shrine|lt_ja=天王神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tennō Shrine (Kameoka)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]][[:en:Kameoka, Kyoto|Kameoka City]]{{ill|Miyamae Town (Kameoka)|ja|宮前町 (亀岡市)|lt=Miyamae Town|lt_ja=宮前町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamae Town (Kameoka)|1=Miyazakichou|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q86733848}} Kamizengen
* {{ill|Tennō Shrine (Kizugawa)|ja|天王神社 (木津川市)|lt=Tennō Shrine|lt_ja=天王神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tennō Shrine (Kizugawa)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]][[:en:Kizugawa, Kyoto|Kizugawa City]] Kizu Kiyomizu
* {{ill|Tennō Shrine (Seika-chō)|ja|天王神社 (精華町)|lt=Tennō Shrine|lt_ja=天王神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tennō Shrine (Seika-chō)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]][[:en:Sōraku District, Kyoto|Sōraku District]][[:en:Seika, Kyoto|Seika Town]] Sugai

[[Category:pages with merged translations]]
==Merged second translation==
__NOTOC__
'''Tennō Shrine''' (天王神社) and '''Tennō-sha''' (天王社) are Shinto shrines of the [[:en:Gion cult|Gion Faith]] tradition that enshrine [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō]] and [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]].

It is observed that this name is used in shrines belonging to the lineage of [[:en:Tsushima Shrine|Tsushima Shrine]] (Tsushima City, Aichi Prefecture). Other shrine names based on the Gion faith include {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|八坂神社 (曖昧さ回避)|lt=Yasaka Shrine|lt_ja=八坂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka Shrine (disambiguation)|qq=}} (also known as {{ill|Yaei Shrine|ja|弥栄神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yaei Shrine|1=Yaei Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487447}}), {{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Gion shrine|WD=Q11588442}}, {{ill|Hiromine Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|広峯神社 (曖昧さ回避)|lt=Hiromine Shrine|lt_ja=広峯神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiromine Shrine (disambiguation)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Yakumo Shrine|ja|八雲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakumo Shrine|1=Yakumo shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391847}}, {{ill|Suga Shrine|ja|須賀神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suga Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Susanoo Shrine|ja|素盞嗚神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo Shrine|qq=}}, names which vary according to the era and source.

Among the current enshrined deities, only a few shrines have [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō]] as their primary deity, while many shrines enshrine [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], who had been syncretized with Gozu Tennō through [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu Shūgō]]. During the Meiji era's separation of Shinto and Buddhism ([[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu Bunri]]), some shrines reverted to the deities recorded in ancient tradition rather than enshrining Susanoo.

Since the Meiji era, due to joint enshrinement ({{ill|Joint Enshrinement|ja|合祀|1=co-Enshrinement|2=ja|3=合祀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Joint Enshrinement|13=User:Immanuelle/co-Enshrinement|qq=}}) with other shrines and the renaming of institutions such as the Tsushima Gozu Tennō Shrine, many shrines have changed their names to, for example, Tsushima Shrine. Additionally, influenced by the separation of Shinto and Buddhism and such joint enshrinement, there are shrines where the enshrined deity is not [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō]], and some where Gozu Tennō is interpreted as a deity other than Susanoo. In certain regions, the legacy of this phenomenon can even be seen in local place names bearing "Tennō."

Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:54, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
__NOTOC__
'''Tenno Jinja''' (天王神社、天王社) is a Shinto shrine dedicated to [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō]] and [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], central deities in [[:en:Gion cult|Gion Faith]]. This name is especially used for shrines in the lineage of [[:en:Tsushima Shrine|Tsushima Shrine]] in [[:en:Tsushima, Aichi|Tsushima City, Aichi Prefecture]]. Other shrines based on Gion Faith include {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|八坂神社 (曖昧さ回避)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka Shrine (disambiguation)|qq=}}, {{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Gion shrine|WD=Q11588442}}, {{ill|Hiromine Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|広峯神社 (曖昧さ回避)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiromine Shrine (disambiguation)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Yakumo Shrine|ja|八雲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakumo Shrine|1=Yakumo shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391847}}, {{ill|Suga Shrine|ja|須賀神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suga Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Susanoo Shrine|ja|素盞嗚神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo Shrine|qq=}}, whose usage varies by era and source.

Currently, only a few shrines still worship Gozu Tennō directly. Most shrines now enshrine [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], who was syncretized with Gozu Tennō under [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]]. During the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) in the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], some shrines reverted to deities listed in ancient records other than Susanoo.

Since the Meiji period, many shrines have been merged with others or renamed to align with the Tsushima Gozu Tennōsha, such as renaming to [[:en:Tsushima Shrine|Tsushima Shrine]]. As a result of Shinbutsu bunri and shrine mergers, some shrines now enshrine deities other than Gozu Tennō or interpret Gozu Tennō as a deity distinct from Susanoo. In some regions, the influence of these shrines can still be seen in place names incorporating "Tenno."

Among the current enshrined deities, only a few shrines have [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō]] as their primary deity, while many shrines enshrine [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], who had been syncretized with Gozu Tennō through [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu Shūgō]]. During the Meiji era's separation of Shinto and Buddhism ([[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu Bunri]]), some shrines reverted to the deities recorded in ancient tradition rather than enshrining Susanoo.

Since the Meiji era, due to joint enshrinement ({{ill|Joint Enshrinement|ja|合祀|1=co-Enshrinement|2=ja|3=合祀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Joint Enshrinement|13=User:Immanuelle/co-Enshrinement|qq=}}) with other shrines and the renaming of institutions such as the Tsushima Gozu Tennō Shrine, many shrines have changed their names to, for example, Tsushima Shrine. Additionally, influenced by the separation of Shinto and Buddhism and such joint enshrinement, there are shrines where the enshrined deity is not [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō]], and some where Gozu Tennō is interpreted as a deity other than Susanoo. In certain regions, the legacy of this phenomenon can even be seen in local place names bearing "Tennō."

Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:54, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
__NOTOC__
'''Tenno Jinja''' (天王神社、天王社) is a Shinto shrine dedicated to [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō]] and [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], central deities in [[:en:Gion cult|Gion Faith]]. This name is especially used for shrines in the lineage of [[:en:Tsushima Shrine|Tsushima Shrine]] in [[:en:Tsushima, Aichi|Tsushima City, Aichi Prefecture]]. Other shrines based on Gion Faith include {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|八坂神社 (曖昧さ回避)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka Shrine (disambiguation)|qq=}}, {{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Gion shrine|WD=Q11588442}}, {{ill|Hiromine Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|広峯神社 (曖昧さ回避)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiromine Shrine (disambiguation)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Yakumo Shrine|ja|八雲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakumo Shrine|1=Yakumo shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391847}}, {{ill|Suga Shrine|ja|須賀神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suga Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Susanoo Shrine|ja|素盞嗚神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo Shrine|qq=}}, whose usage varies by era and source.

Currently, only a few shrines still worship Gozu Tennō directly. Most shrines now enshrine [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], who was syncretized with Gozu Tennō under [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]]. During the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) in the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], some shrines reverted to deities listed in ancient records other than Susanoo.

Since the Meiji period, many shrines have been merged with others or renamed to align with the Tsushima Gozu Tennōsha, such as renaming to [[:en:Tsushima Shrine|Tsushima Shrine]]. As a result of Shinbutsu bunri and shrine mergers, some shrines now enshrine deities other than Gozu Tennō or interpret Gozu Tennō as a deity distinct from Susanoo. In some regions, the influence of these shrines can still be seen in place names incorporating "Tenno."

* {{ill|Susanoo Shrine (Shibata)|ja|須佐之男神社 (新発田市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo Shrine (Shibata)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Tenno Shrine) - Niigata Prefecture, Shibata, {{ill|Tenno (Shibata)|ja|天王 (新発田市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenno (Shibata)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref>[http://www.niigata-kankou.or.jp/sys/data?page-id=10163], [http://www.niigata-u.com/files/ngt2009g/091010b1.html], [http://shibata-info.jp/archives/eventcal/%E5%A4%A9%E7%8E%8B%E3%81%BE%E3%81%A4%E3%82%8A]</ref>

* {{ill|Tenno Shrine Hachiman Shrine|ja|天王社八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenno Shrine Hachiman Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Tenno Shrine (Kizugawa)|ja|天王神社 (木津川市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenno Shrine (Kizugawa)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Kyoto Prefecture, Kizugawa, {{ill|Kizu Shimizu|ja|木津清水|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kizu Shimizu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

'''Tennō Shrine''' (天王神社) and '''Tennō-sha''' (天王社) are Shinto shrines of the [[:en:Gion cult|Gion Faith]] tradition that enshrine [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō]] and [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]].

It is observed that this name is used in shrines belonging to the lineage of [[:en:Tsushima Shrine|Tsushima Shrine]] (Tsushima City, Aichi Prefecture). Other shrine names based on the Gion faith include {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|八坂神社 (曖昧さ回避)|lt=Yasaka Shrine|lt_ja=八坂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka Shrine (disambiguation)|qq=}} (also known as {{ill|Yaei Shrine|ja|弥栄神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yaei Shrine|1=Yaei Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487447}}), {{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Gion shrine|WD=Q11588442}}, {{ill|Hiromine Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|広峯神社 (曖昧さ回避)|lt=Hiromine Shrine|lt_ja=広峯神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiromine Shrine (disambiguation)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Yakumo Shrine|ja|八雲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakumo Shrine|1=Yakumo shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391847}}, {{ill|Suga Shrine|ja|須賀神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suga Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Susanoo Shrine|ja|素盞嗚神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo Shrine|qq=}}, names which vary according to the era and source.

Among the current enshrined deities, only a few shrines have [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō]] as their primary deity, while many shrines enshrine [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], who had been syncretized with Gozu Tennō through [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu Shūgō]]. During the Meiji era's separation of Shinto and Buddhism ([[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu Bunri]]), some shrines reverted to the deities recorded in ancient tradition rather than enshrining Susanoo.

Since the Meiji era, due to joint enshrinement ({{ill|Joint Enshrinement|ja|合祀|1=co-Enshrinement|2=ja|3=合祀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Joint Enshrinement|13=User:Immanuelle/co-Enshrinement|qq=}}) with other shrines and the renaming of institutions such as the Tsushima Gozu Tennō Shrine, many shrines have changed their names to, for example, Tsushima Shrine. Additionally, influenced by the separation of Shinto and Buddhism and such joint enshrinement, there are shrines where the enshrined deity is not [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō]], and some where Gozu Tennō is interpreted as a deity other than Susanoo. In certain regions, the legacy of this phenomenon can even be seen in local place names bearing "Tennō."

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:天王神社]]
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11443028]]
Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:54, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
__NOTOC__
'''Tenno Jinja''' (天王神社、天王社) is a Shinto shrine dedicated to [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō]] and [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], central deities in [[:en:Gion cult|Gion Faith]]. This name is especially used for shrines in the lineage of [[:en:Tsushima Shrine|Tsushima Shrine]] in [[:en:Tsushima, Aichi|Tsushima City, Aichi Prefecture]]. Other shrines based on Gion Faith include {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|八坂神社 (曖昧さ回避)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka Shrine (disambiguation)|qq=}}, {{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Gion shrine|WD=Q11588442}}, {{ill|Hiromine Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|広峯神社 (曖昧さ回避)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiromine Shrine (disambiguation)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Yakumo Shrine|ja|八雲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakumo Shrine|1=Yakumo shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391847}}, {{ill|Suga Shrine|ja|須賀神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suga Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Susanoo Shrine|ja|素盞嗚神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo Shrine|qq=}}, whose usage varies by era and source.

Currently, only a few shrines still worship Gozu Tennō directly. Most shrines now enshrine [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], who was syncretized with Gozu Tennō under [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]]. During the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) in the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], some shrines reverted to deities listed in ancient records other than Susanoo.

Since the Meiji period, many shrines have been merged with others or renamed to align with the Tsushima Gozu Tennōsha, such as renaming to [[:en:Tsushima Shrine|Tsushima Shrine]]. As a result of Shinbutsu bunri and shrine mergers, some shrines now enshrine deities other than Gozu Tennō or interpret Gozu Tennō as a deity distinct from Susanoo. In some regions, the influence of these shrines can still be seen in place names incorporating "Tenno."

== List ==
* {{ill|Tenno Shrine (Shichinohe)|ja|天王神社 (七戸町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenno Shrine (Shichinohe)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Aomori Prefecture, Kamikita District, Shichinohe, {{ill|Shichinohe|ja|七戸 (七戸町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichinohe|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Susanoo Shrine (Shibata)|ja|須佐之男神社 (新発田市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo Shrine (Shibata)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Tenno Shrine) - Niigata Prefecture, Shibata, {{ill|Tenno (Shibata)|ja|天王 (新発田市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenno (Shibata)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref>[http://www.niigata-kankou.or.jp/sys/data?page-id=10163], [http://www.niigata-u.com/files/ngt2009g/091010b1.html], [http://shibata-info.jp/archives/eventcal/%E5%A4%A9%E7%8E%8B%E3%81%BE%E3%81%A4%E3%82%8A]</ref>
* {{ill|Tenno Shrine (Kameoka)|ja|天王神社 (亀岡市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenno Shrine (Kameoka)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Kyoto Prefecture, Kameoka, {{ill|Miyamae Town|ja|宮前町 (亀岡市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamae Town|1=Miyazakichou|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q86733848}}, Kamisaki, Tenno Shita<ref>[http://agua.jpn.org/film/f99.html], [http://901-1000.wakkan.jp/927.html]</ref>
* {{ill|Tenno Shrine Hachiman Shrine|ja|天王社八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenno Shrine Hachiman Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Tenno Shrine (Kizugawa)|ja|天王神社 (木津川市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenno Shrine (Kizugawa)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Kyoto Prefecture, Kizugawa, {{ill|Kizu Shimizu|ja|木津清水|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kizu Shimizu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Tenno Shrine (Komatsushima)|ja|天王社 (小松島市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenno Shrine (Komatsushima)|qq=}} - Tokushima Prefecture, Komatsushima, {{ill|Tano Town (Komatsushima)|ja|田野町 (小松島市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tano Town (Komatsushima)|1=Tano, Komatsushima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577483}}
