* {{ill|Tennō Shrine (Hashimoto Yagurawaki)|ja|天王神社 (橋本市矢倉脇)|lt=Tennō Shrine|lt_ja=天王神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tennō Shrine (Hashimoto Yagurawaki)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]][[:en:Hashimoto, Wakayama|Hashimoto City]] Yagurawaki
* {{ill|Tennō Hachiman Shrine|ja|天王八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tennō Hachiman Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]][[:en:Niimi|Niimi City]][[:en:Tetta, Okayama|Tetsuta Town]]

During the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) in the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], some shrines reverted to deities listed in ancient records other than Susanoo.

Since the Meiji period, many shrines have been merged with others or renamed to align with the Tsushima Gozu Tennōsha, such as renaming to [[:en:Tsushima Shrine|Tsushima Shrine]]. As a result of Shinbutsu bunri and shrine mergers, some shrines now enshrine deities other than Gozu Tennō or interpret Gozu Tennō as a deity distinct from Susanoo. In some regions, the influence of these shrines can still be seen in place names incorporating "Tenno."

==Merged second translation==
__NOTOC__
'''Tennō Shrine''' (天王神社) and '''Tennō-sha''' (天王社) are Shinto shrines of the [[:en:Gion cult|Gion Faith]] tradition that enshrine [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō]] and [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]].

It is observed that this name is used in shrines belonging to the lineage of [[:en:Tsushima Shrine|Tsushima Shrine]] (Tsushima City, Aichi Prefecture). Other shrine names based on the Gion faith include {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|八坂神社 (曖昧さ回避)|lt=Yasaka Shrine|lt_ja=八坂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka Shrine (disambiguation)|qq=}} (also known as {{ill|Yaei Shrine|ja|弥栄神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yaei Shrine|1=Yaei Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487447}}), {{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Gion shrine|WD=Q11588442}}, {{ill|Hiromine Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|広峯神社 (曖昧さ回避)|lt=Hiromine Shrine|lt_ja=広峯神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiromine Shrine (disambiguation)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Yakumo Shrine|ja|八雲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakumo Shrine|1=Yakumo shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391847}}, {{ill|Suga Shrine|ja|須賀神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suga Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Susanoo Shrine|ja|素盞嗚神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo Shrine|qq=}}, names which vary according to the era and source.

Among the current enshrined deities, only a few shrines have [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō]] as their primary deity, while many shrines enshrine [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], who had been syncretized with Gozu Tennō through [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu Shūgō]]. During the Meiji era's separation of Shinto and Buddhism ([[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu Bunri]]), some shrines reverted to the deities recorded in ancient tradition rather than enshrining Susanoo.

Since the Meiji era, due to joint enshrinement ({{ill|Joint Enshrinement|ja|合祀|1=co-Enshrinement|2=ja|3=合祀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Joint Enshrinement|13=User:Immanuelle/co-Enshrinement|qq=}}) with other shrines and the renaming of institutions such as the Tsushima Gozu Tennō Shrine, many shrines have changed their names to, for example, Tsushima Shrine. Additionally, influenced by the separation of Shinto and Buddhism and such joint enshrinement, there are shrines where the enshrined deity is not [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō]], and some where Gozu Tennō is interpreted as a deity other than Susanoo. In certain regions, the legacy of this phenomenon can even be seen in local place names bearing "Tennō."

__NOTOC__
'''Tenno Jinja''' (天王神社、天王社) is a Shinto shrine dedicated to [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō]] and [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], central deities in [[:en:Gion cult|Gion Faith]]. This name is especially used for shrines in the lineage of [[:en:Tsushima Shrine|Tsushima Shrine]] in [[:en:Tsushima, Aichi|Tsushima City, Aichi Prefecture]]. Other shrines based on Gion Faith include {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|八坂神社 (曖昧さ回避)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka Shrine (disambiguation)|qq=}}, {{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Gion shrine|WD=Q11588442}}, {{ill|Hiromine Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|広峯神社 (曖昧さ回避)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiromine Shrine (disambiguation)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Yakumo Shrine|ja|八雲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakumo Shrine|1=Yakumo shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391847}}, {{ill|Suga Shrine|ja|須賀神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suga Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Susanoo Shrine|ja|素盞嗚神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo Shrine|qq=}}, whose usage varies by era and source.

Currently, only a few shrines still worship Gozu Tennō directly. Most shrines now enshrine [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], who was syncretized with Gozu Tennō under [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]]. During the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) in the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], some shrines reverted to deities listed in ancient records other than Susanoo.

Since the Meiji period, many shrines have been merged with others or renamed to align with the Tsushima Gozu Tennōsha, such as renaming to [[:en:Tsushima Shrine|Tsushima Shrine]]. As a result of Shinbutsu bunri and shrine mergers, some shrines now enshrine deities other than Gozu Tennō or interpret Gozu Tennō as a deity distinct from Susanoo. In some regions, the influence of these shrines can still be seen in place names incorporating "Tenno."

== List ==

'''Tennō Shrine''' (天王神社) and '''Tennō-sha''' (天王社) are Shinto shrines of the [[:en:Gion cult|Gion Faith]] tradition that enshrine [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō]] and [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]].

It is observed that this name is used in shrines belonging to the lineage of [[:en:Tsushima Shrine|Tsushima Shrine]] (Tsushima City, Aichi Prefecture). Other shrine names based on the Gion faith include {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|八坂神社 (曖昧さ回避)|lt=Yasaka Shrine|lt_ja=八坂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka Shrine (disambiguation)|qq=}} (also known as {{ill|Yaei Shrine|ja|弥栄神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yaei Shrine|1=Yaei Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487447}}), {{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Gion shrine|WD=Q11588442}}, {{ill|Hiromine Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|広峯神社 (曖昧さ回避)|lt=Hiromine Shrine|lt_ja=広峯神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiromine Shrine (disambiguation)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Yakumo Shrine|ja|八雲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakumo Shrine|1=Yakumo shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391847}}, {{ill|Suga Shrine|ja|須賀神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suga Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Susanoo Shrine|ja|素盞嗚神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo Shrine|qq=}}, names which vary according to the era and source.

Among the current enshrined deities, only a few shrines have [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō]] as their primary deity, while many shrines enshrine [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], who had been syncretized with Gozu Tennō through [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu Shūgō]]. During the Meiji era's separation of Shinto and Buddhism ([[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu Bunri]]), some shrines reverted to the deities recorded in ancient tradition rather than enshrining Susanoo.

Since the Meiji era, due to joint enshrinement ({{ill|Joint Enshrinement|ja|合祀|1=co-Enshrinement|2=ja|3=合祀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Joint Enshrinement|13=User:Immanuelle/co-Enshrinement|qq=}}) with other shrines and the renaming of institutions such as the Tsushima Gozu Tennō Shrine, many shrines have changed their names to, for example, Tsushima Shrine. Additionally, influenced by the separation of Shinto and Buddhism and such joint enshrinement, there are shrines where the enshrined deity is not [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō]], and some where Gozu Tennō is interpreted as a deity other than Susanoo. In certain regions, the legacy of this phenomenon can even be seen in local place names bearing "Tennō."

__NOTOC__
'''Tenno Jinja''' (天王神社、天王社) is a Shinto shrine dedicated to [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō]] and [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], central deities in [[:en:Gion cult|Gion Faith]]. This name is especially used for shrines in the lineage of [[:en:Tsushima Shrine|Tsushima Shrine]] in [[:en:Tsushima, Aichi|Tsushima City, Aichi Prefecture]]. Other shrines based on Gion Faith include {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|八坂神社 (曖昧さ回避)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka Shrine (disambiguation)|qq=}}, {{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Gion shrine|WD=Q11588442}}, {{ill|Hiromine Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|広峯神社 (曖昧さ回避)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiromine Shrine (disambiguation)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Yakumo Shrine|ja|八雲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakumo Shrine|1=Yakumo shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391847}}, {{ill|Suga Shrine|ja|須賀神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suga Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Susanoo Shrine|ja|素盞嗚神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo Shrine|qq=}}, whose usage varies by era and source.

Currently, only a few shrines still worship Gozu Tennō directly. Most shrines now enshrine [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], who was syncretized with Gozu Tennō under [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]]. During the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) in the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], some shrines reverted to deities listed in ancient records other than Susanoo.

Since the Meiji period, many shrines have been merged with others or renamed to align with the Tsushima Gozu Tennōsha, such as renaming to [[:en:Tsushima Shrine|Tsushima Shrine]]. As a result of Shinbutsu bunri and shrine mergers, some shrines now enshrine deities other than Gozu Tennō or interpret Gozu Tennō as a deity distinct from Susanoo. In some regions, the influence of these shrines can still be seen in place names incorporating "Tenno."

* {{ill|Susanoo Shrine (Shibata)|ja|須佐之男神社 (新発田市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo Shrine (Shibata)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Tenno Shrine) - Niigata Prefecture, Shibata, {{ill|Tenno (Shibata)|ja|天王 (新発田市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenno (Shibata)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref>[http://www.niigata-kankou.or.jp/sys/data?page-id=10163], [http://www.niigata-u.com/files/ngt2009g/091010b1.html], [http://shibata-info.jp/archives/eventcal/%E5%A4%A9%E7%8E%8B%E3%81%BE%E3%81%A4%E3%82%8A]</ref>
* {{ill|Tenno Shrine (Kameoka)|ja|天王神社 (亀岡市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenno Shrine (Kameoka)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Kyoto Prefecture, Kameoka, {{ill|Miyamae Town|ja|宮前町 (亀岡市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamae Town|1=Miyazakichou|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q86733848}}, Kamisaki, Tenno Shita<ref>[http://agua.jpn.org/film/f99.html], [http://901-1000.wakkan.jp/927.html]</ref>
* {{ill|Tenno Shrine Hachiman Shrine|ja|天王社八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenno Shrine Hachiman Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Tenno Shrine (Kizugawa)|ja|天王神社 (木津川市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenno Shrine (Kizugawa)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Kyoto Prefecture, Kizugawa, {{ill|Kizu Shimizu|ja|木津清水|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kizu Shimizu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:天王神社]]
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:天王神社]]
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:天王神社]]
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11443028]]
Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:54, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
__NOTOC__
'''Tenno Jinja''' (天王神社、天王社) is a Shinto shrine dedicated to {{ill|Gozu Tennō|en|Gozu Tennō|WD=Q11570247|lt=Gozu Tennō}} and {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, central deities in {{ill|Gion cult|en|Gion cult|WD=Q11588425|lt=Gion Faith}}. This name is especially used for shrines in the lineage of {{ill|Tsushima Shrine|en|Tsushima Shrine|WD=Q705136|lt=Tsushima Shrine}} in {{ill|Tsushima, Aichi|en|Tsushima, Aichi|WD=Q816736|lt=Tsushima City, Aichi Prefecture}}. Other shrines based on Gion Faith include {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|八坂神社 (曖昧さ回避)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka Shrine (disambiguation)|qq=}}, {{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Gion shrine|WD=Q11588442}}, {{ill|Hiromine Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|広峯神社 (曖昧さ回避)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiromine Shrine (disambiguation)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Yakumo Shrine|ja|八雲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakumo Shrine|1=Yakumo shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391847}}, {{ill|Suga Shrine|ja|須賀神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suga Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Susanoo Shrine|ja|素盞嗚神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo Shrine|qq=}}, whose usage varies by era and source.

Currently, only a few shrines still worship Gozu Tennō directly. Most shrines now enshrine {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, who was syncretized with Gozu Tennō under {{ill|Shinbutsu-shūgō|en|Shinbutsu-shūgō|WD=Q2279088|lt=Shinbutsu-shūgō}}. During the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|WD=Q1970535|lt=Shinbutsu bunri}} (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) in the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}}, some shrines reverted to deities listed in ancient records other than Susanoo.

Since the Meiji period, many shrines have been merged with others or renamed to align with the Tsushima Gozu Tennōsha, such as renaming to {{ill|Tsushima Shrine|en|Tsushima Shrine|WD=Q705136|lt=Tsushima Shrine}}. As a result of Shinbutsu bunri and shrine mergers, some shrines now enshrine deities other than Gozu Tennō or interpret Gozu Tennō as a deity distinct from Susanoo. In some regions, the influence of these shrines can still be seen in place names incorporating "Tenno."

* {{ill|Susanoo Shrine (Shibata)|ja|須佐之男神社 (新発田市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo Shrine (Shibata)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Tenno Shrine) - Niigata Prefecture, Shibata, {{ill|Tenno (Shibata)|ja|天王 (新発田市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenno (Shibata)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref>[http://www.niigata-kankou.or.jp/sys/data?page-id=10163], [http://www.niigata-u.com/files/ngt2009g/091010b1.html], [http://shibata-info.jp/archives/eventcal/%E5%A4%A9%E7%8E%8B%E3%81%BE%E3%81%A4%E3%82%8A]</ref>

* {{ill|Tenno Shrine (Kizugawa)|ja|天王神社 (木津川市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenno Shrine (Kizugawa)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Kyoto Prefecture, Kizugawa, {{ill|Kizu Shimizu|ja|木津清水|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kizu Shimizu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

==Merged second translation==
__NOTOC__
'''Tennō Shrine''' (天王神社) and '''Tennō-sha''' (天王社) are Shinto shrines of the {{ill|Gion cult|en|Gion cult|WD=Q11588425|lt=Gion Faith}} tradition that enshrine {{ill|Gozu Tennō|en|Gozu Tennō|WD=Q11570247|lt=Gozu Tennō}} and {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}.

It is observed that this name is used in shrines belonging to the lineage of {{ill|Tsushima Shrine|en|Tsushima Shrine|WD=Q705136|lt=Tsushima Shrine}} (Tsushima City, Aichi Prefecture). Other shrine names based on the Gion faith include {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|八坂神社 (曖昧さ回避)|lt=Yasaka Shrine|lt_ja=八坂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka Shrine (disambiguation)|qq=}} (also known as {{ill|Yaei Shrine|ja|弥栄神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yaei Shrine|1=Yaei Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487447}}), {{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Gion shrine|WD=Q11588442}}, {{ill|Hiromine Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|広峯神社 (曖昧さ回避)|lt=Hiromine Shrine|lt_ja=広峯神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiromine Shrine (disambiguation)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Yakumo Shrine|ja|八雲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakumo Shrine|1=Yakumo shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391847}}, {{ill|Suga Shrine|ja|須賀神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suga Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Susanoo Shrine|ja|素盞嗚神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo Shrine|qq=}}, names which vary according to the era and source.

Among the current enshrined deities, only a few shrines have {{ill|Gozu Tennō|en|Gozu Tennō|WD=Q11570247|lt=Gozu Tennō}} as their primary deity, while many shrines enshrine {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, who had been syncretized with Gozu Tennō through {{ill|Shinbutsu-shūgō|en|Shinbutsu-shūgō|WD=Q2279088|lt=Shinbutsu Shūgō}}. During the Meiji era's separation of Shinto and Buddhism ({{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|WD=Q1970535|lt=Shinbutsu Bunri}}), some shrines reverted to the deities recorded in ancient tradition rather than enshrining Susanoo.

Since the Meiji era, due to joint enshrinement ({{ill|Joint Enshrinement|ja|合祀|1=co-Enshrinement|2=ja|3=合祀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Joint Enshrinement|13=User:Immanuelle/co-Enshrinement|qq=}}) with other shrines and the renaming of institutions such as the Tsushima Gozu Tennō Shrine, many shrines have changed their names to, for example, Tsushima Shrine. Additionally, influenced by the separation of Shinto and Buddhism and such joint enshrinement, there are shrines where the enshrined deity is not {{ill|Gozu Tennō|en|Gozu Tennō|WD=Q11570247|lt=Gozu Tennō}}, and some where Gozu Tennō is interpreted as a deity other than Susanoo. In certain regions, the legacy of this phenomenon can even be seen in local place names bearing "Tennō."

Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:54, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
__NOTOC__
'''Tenno Jinja''' (天王神社、天王社) is a Shinto shrine dedicated to {{ill|Gozu Tennō|en|Gozu Tennō|WD=Q11570247|lt=Gozu Tennō}} and {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, central deities in {{ill|Gion cult|en|Gion cult|WD=Q11588425|lt=Gion Faith}}. This name is especially used for shrines in the lineage of {{ill|Tsushima Shrine|en|Tsushima Shrine|WD=Q705136|lt=Tsushima Shrine}} in {{ill|Tsushima, Aichi|en|Tsushima, Aichi|WD=Q816736|lt=Tsushima City, Aichi Prefecture}}. Other shrines based on Gion Faith include {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|八坂神社 (曖昧さ回避)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka Shrine (disambiguation)|qq=}}, {{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Gion shrine|WD=Q11588442}}, {{ill|Hiromine Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|広峯神社 (曖昧さ回避)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiromine Shrine (disambiguation)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Yakumo Shrine|ja|八雲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakumo Shrine|1=Yakumo shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391847}}, {{ill|Suga Shrine|ja|須賀神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suga Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Susanoo Shrine|ja|素盞嗚神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo Shrine|qq=}}, whose usage varies by era and source.

Currently, only a few shrines still worship Gozu Tennō directly. Most shrines now enshrine {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, who was syncretized with Gozu Tennō under {{ill|Shinbutsu-shūgō|en|Shinbutsu-shūgō|WD=Q2279088|lt=Shinbutsu-shūgō}}. During the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|WD=Q1970535|lt=Shinbutsu bunri}} (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) in the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}}, some shrines reverted to deities listed in ancient records other than Susanoo.

Since the Meiji period, many shrines have been merged with others or renamed to align with the Tsushima Gozu Tennōsha, such as renaming to {{ill|Tsushima Shrine|en|Tsushima Shrine|WD=Q705136|lt=Tsushima Shrine}}. As a result of Shinbutsu bunri and shrine mergers, some shrines now enshrine deities other than Gozu Tennō or interpret Gozu Tennō as a deity distinct from Susanoo. In some regions, the influence of these shrines can still be seen in place names incorporating "Tenno."

* {{ill|Susanoo Shrine (Shibata)|ja|須佐之男神社 (新発田市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo Shrine (Shibata)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Tenno Shrine) - Niigata Prefecture, Shibata, {{ill|Tenno (Shibata)|ja|天王 (新発田市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenno (Shibata)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref>[http://www.niigata-kankou.or.jp/sys/data?page-id=10163], [http://www.niigata-u.com/files/ngt2009g/091010b1.html], [http://shibata-info.jp/archives/eventcal/%E5%A4%A9%E7%8E%8B%E3%81%BE%E3%81%A4%E3%82%8A]</ref>

* {{ill|Tenno Shrine (Kizugawa)|ja|天王神社 (木津川市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenno Shrine (Kizugawa)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Kyoto Prefecture, Kizugawa, {{ill|Kizu Shimizu|ja|木津清水|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kizu Shimizu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

==Merged second translation==
__NOTOC__
'''Tennō Shrine''' (天王神社) and '''Tennō-sha''' (天王社) are Shinto shrines of the {{ill|Gion cult|en|Gion cult|WD=Q11588425|lt=Gion Faith}} tradition that enshrine {{ill|Gozu Tennō|en|Gozu Tennō|WD=Q11570247|lt=Gozu Tennō}} and {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}.

It is observed that this name is used in shrines belonging to the lineage of {{ill|Tsushima Shrine|en|Tsushima Shrine|WD=Q705136|lt=Tsushima Shrine}} (Tsushima City, Aichi Prefecture). Other shrine names based on the Gion faith include {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|八坂神社 (曖昧さ回避)|lt=Yasaka Shrine|lt_ja=八坂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka Shrine (disambiguation)|qq=}} (also known as {{ill|Yaei Shrine|ja|弥栄神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yaei Shrine|1=Yaei Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487447}}), {{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Gion shrine|WD=Q11588442}}, {{ill|Hiromine Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|広峯神社 (曖昧さ回避)|lt=Hiromine Shrine|lt_ja=広峯神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiromine Shrine (disambiguation)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Yakumo Shrine|ja|八雲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakumo Shrine|1=Yakumo shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391847}}, {{ill|Suga Shrine|ja|須賀神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suga Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Susanoo Shrine|ja|素盞嗚神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo Shrine|qq=}}, names which vary according to the era and source.

Among the current enshrined deities, only a few shrines have {{ill|Gozu Tennō|en|Gozu Tennō|WD=Q11570247|lt=Gozu Tennō}} as their primary deity, while many shrines enshrine {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, who had been syncretized with Gozu Tennō through {{ill|Shinbutsu-shūgō|en|Shinbutsu-shūgō|WD=Q2279088|lt=Shinbutsu Shūgō}}. During the Meiji era's separation of Shinto and Buddhism ({{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|WD=Q1970535|lt=Shinbutsu Bunri}}), some shrines reverted to the deities recorded in ancient tradition rather than enshrining Susanoo.

Since the Meiji era, due to joint enshrinement ({{ill|Joint Enshrinement|ja|合祀|1=co-Enshrinement|2=ja|3=合祀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Joint Enshrinement|13=User:Immanuelle/co-Enshrinement|qq=}}) with other shrines and the renaming of institutions such as the Tsushima Gozu Tennō Shrine, many shrines have changed their names to, for example, Tsushima Shrine. Additionally, influenced by the separation of Shinto and Buddhism and such joint enshrinement, there are shrines where the enshrined deity is not {{ill|Gozu Tennō|en|Gozu Tennō|WD=Q11570247|lt=Gozu Tennō}}, and some where Gozu Tennō is interpreted as a deity other than Susanoo. In certain regions, the legacy of this phenomenon can even be seen in local place names bearing "Tennō."

Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:54, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
__NOTOC__
'''Tenno Jinja''' (天王神社、天王社) is a Shinto shrine dedicated to {{ill|Gozu Tennō|en|Gozu Tennō|WD=Q11570247|lt=Gozu Tennō}} and {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, central deities in {{ill|Gion cult|en|Gion cult|WD=Q11588425|lt=Gion Faith}}. This name is especially used for shrines in the lineage of {{ill|Tsushima Shrine|en|Tsushima Shrine|WD=Q705136|lt=Tsushima Shrine}} in {{ill|Tsushima, Aichi|en|Tsushima, Aichi|WD=Q816736|lt=Tsushima City, Aichi Prefecture}}. Other shrines based on Gion Faith include {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|八坂神社 (曖昧さ回避)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka Shrine (disambiguation)|qq=}}, {{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Gion shrine|WD=Q11588442}}, {{ill|Hiromine Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|広峯神社 (曖昧さ回避)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiromine Shrine (disambiguation)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Yakumo Shrine|ja|八雲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakumo Shrine|1=Yakumo shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391847}}, {{ill|Suga Shrine|ja|須賀神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suga Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Susanoo Shrine|ja|素盞嗚神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo Shrine|qq=}}, whose usage varies by era and source.

Currently, only a few shrines still worship Gozu Tennō directly. Most shrines now enshrine {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, who was syncretized with Gozu Tennō under {{ill|Shinbutsu-shūgō|en|Shinbutsu-shūgō|WD=Q2279088|lt=Shinbutsu-shūgō}}. During the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|WD=Q1970535|lt=Shinbutsu bunri}} (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) in the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}}, some shrines reverted to deities listed in ancient records other than Susanoo.

Since the Meiji period, many shrines have been merged with others or renamed to align with the Tsushima Gozu Tennōsha, such as renaming to {{ill|Tsushima Shrine|en|Tsushima Shrine|WD=Q705136|lt=Tsushima Shrine}}. As a result of Shinbutsu bunri and shrine mergers, some shrines now enshrine deities other than Gozu Tennō or interpret Gozu Tennō as a deity distinct from Susanoo. In some regions, the influence of these shrines can still be seen in place names incorporating "Tenno."

== List ==
* {{ill|Tenno Shrine (Shichinohe)|ja|天王神社 (七戸町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenno Shrine (Shichinohe)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Aomori Prefecture, Kamikita District, Shichinohe, {{ill|Shichinohe|ja|七戸 (七戸町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichinohe|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Susanoo Shrine (Shibata)|ja|須佐之男神社 (新発田市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo Shrine (Shibata)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Tenno Shrine) - Niigata Prefecture, Shibata, {{ill|Tenno (Shibata)|ja|天王 (新発田市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenno (Shibata)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref>[http://www.niigata-kankou.or.jp/sys/data?page-id=10163], [http://www.niigata-u.com/files/ngt2009g/091010b1.html], [http://shibata-info.jp/archives/eventcal/%E5%A4%A9%E7%8E%8B%E3%81%BE%E3%81%A4%E3%82%8A]</ref>
* {{ill|Tenno Shrine (Kameoka)|ja|天王神社 (亀岡市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenno Shrine (Kameoka)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Kyoto Prefecture, Kameoka, {{ill|Miyamae Town|ja|宮前町 (亀岡市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamae Town|1=Miyazakichou|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q86733848}}, Kamisaki, Tenno Shita<ref>[http://agua.jpn.org/film/f99.html], [http://901-1000.wakkan.jp/927.html]</ref>
* {{ill|Tenno Shrine Hachiman Shrine|ja|天王社八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenno Shrine Hachiman Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Tenno Shrine (Kizugawa)|ja|天王神社 (木津川市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenno Shrine (Kizugawa)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Kyoto Prefecture, Kizugawa, {{ill|Kizu Shimizu|ja|木津清水|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kizu Shimizu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Tenno Shrine (Komatsushima)|ja|天王社 (小松島市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenno Shrine (Komatsushima)|qq=}} - Tokushima Prefecture, Komatsushima, {{ill|Tano Town (Komatsushima)|ja|田野町 (小松島市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tano Town (Komatsushima)|1=Tano, Komatsushima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577483}}
* {{ill|Gozu Tennō|en|Gozu Tennō|WD=Q11570247|lt=Gozu Tennō神社}}
* {{ill|Tenno Hachiman Shrine|ja|天王八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenno Hachiman Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Kenoda Suga Shrine|ja|健田須賀神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenoda Suga Shrine|1=Takedasuga Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11387225}}
* {{ill|Tennosha Shrine (Komatsushima City)|ja|天王社 (小松島市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tennosha Shrine (Komatsushima City)|qq=}}

==Merged second translation==
__NOTOC__
'''Tennō Shrine''' (天王神社) and '''Tennō-sha''' (天王社) are Shinto shrines of the {{ill|Gion cult|en|Gion cult|WD=Q11588425|lt=Gion Faith}} tradition that enshrine {{ill|Gozu Tennō|en|Gozu Tennō|WD=Q11570247|lt=Gozu Tennō}} and {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}.

It is observed that this name is used in shrines belonging to the lineage of {{ill|Tsushima Shrine|en|Tsushima Shrine|WD=Q705136|lt=Tsushima Shrine}} (Tsushima City, Aichi Prefecture). Other shrine names based on the Gion faith include {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|八坂神社 (曖昧さ回避)|lt=Yasaka Shrine|lt_ja=八坂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka Shrine (disambiguation)|qq=}} (also known as {{ill|Yaei Shrine|ja|弥栄神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yaei Shrine|1=Yaei Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487447}}), {{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Gion shrine|WD=Q11588442}}, {{ill|Hiromine Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|広峯神社 (曖昧さ回避)|lt=Hiromine Shrine|lt_ja=広峯神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiromine Shrine (disambiguation)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Yakumo Shrine|ja|八雲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakumo Shrine|1=Yakumo shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391847}}, {{ill|Suga Shrine|ja|須賀神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suga Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Susanoo Shrine|ja|素盞嗚神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo Shrine|qq=}}, names which vary according to the era and source.

Among the current enshrined deities, only a few shrines have {{ill|Gozu Tennō|en|Gozu Tennō|WD=Q11570247|lt=Gozu Tennō}} as their primary deity, while many shrines enshrine {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, who had been syncretized with Gozu Tennō through {{ill|Shinbutsu-shūgō|en|Shinbutsu-shūgō|WD=Q2279088|lt=Shinbutsu Shūgō}}. During the Meiji era's separation of Shinto and Buddhism ({{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|WD=Q1970535|lt=Shinbutsu Bunri}}), some shrines reverted to the deities recorded in ancient tradition rather than enshrining Susanoo.

Since the Meiji era, due to joint enshrinement ({{ill|Joint Enshrinement|ja|合祀|1=co-Enshrinement|2=ja|3=合祀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Joint Enshrinement|13=User:Immanuelle/co-Enshrinement|qq=}}) with other shrines and the renaming of institutions such as the Tsushima Gozu Tennō Shrine, many shrines have changed their names to, for example, Tsushima Shrine. Additionally, influenced by the separation of Shinto and Buddhism and such joint enshrinement, there are shrines where the enshrined deity is not {{ill|Gozu Tennō|en|Gozu Tennō|WD=Q11570247|lt=Gozu Tennō}}, and some where Gozu Tennō is interpreted as a deity other than Susanoo. In certain regions, the legacy of this phenomenon can even be seen in local place names bearing "Tennō."

'''Tenno Jinja''' (天王神社、天王社) is a Shinto shrine dedicated to {{ill|Gozu Tennō|en|Gozu Tennō|WD=Q11570247|lt=Gozu Tennō}} and {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, central deities in {{ill|Gion cult|en|Gion cult|WD=Q11588425|lt=Gion Faith}}. This name is especially used for shrines in the lineage of {{ill|Tsushima Shrine|en|Tsushima Shrine|WD=Q705136|lt=Tsushima Shrine}} in {{ill|Tsushima, Aichi|en|Tsushima, Aichi|WD=Q816736|lt=Tsushima City, Aichi Prefecture}}. Other shrines based on Gion Faith include {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|八坂神社 (曖昧さ回避)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka Shrine (disambiguation)|qq=}}, {{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Gion shrine|WD=Q11588442}}, {{ill|Hiromine Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|広峯神社 (曖昧さ回避)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiromine Shrine (disambiguation)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Yakumo Shrine|ja|八雲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakumo Shrine|1=Yakumo shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391847}}, {{ill|Suga Shrine|ja|須賀神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suga Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Susanoo Shrine|ja|素盞嗚神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo Shrine|qq=}}, whose usage varies by era and source.

Currently, only a few shrines still worship Gozu Tennō directly. Most shrines now enshrine {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, who was syncretized with Gozu Tennō under {{ill|Shinbutsu-shūgō|en|Shinbutsu-shūgō|WD=Q2279088|lt=Shinbutsu-shūgō}}. During the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|WD=Q1970535|lt=Shinbutsu bunri}} (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) in the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}}, some shrines reverted to deities listed in ancient records other than Susanoo.

Since the Meiji period, many shrines have been merged with others or renamed to align with the Tsushima Gozu Tennōsha, such as renaming to {{ill|Tsushima Shrine|en|Tsushima Shrine|WD=Q705136|lt=Tsushima Shrine}}. As a result of Shinbutsu bunri and shrine mergers, some shrines now enshrine deities other than Gozu Tennō or interpret Gozu Tennō as a deity distinct from Susanoo. In some regions, the influence of these shrines can still be seen in place names incorporating "Tenno."

==Merged second translation==
__NOTOC__
'''Tennō Shrine''' (天王神社) and '''Tennō-sha''' (天王社) are Shinto shrines of the {{ill|Gion cult|en|Gion cult|WD=Q11588425|lt=Gion Faith}} tradition that enshrine {{ill|Gozu Tennō|en|Gozu Tennō|WD=Q11570247|lt=Gozu Tennō}} and {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}.

It is observed that this name is used in shrines belonging to the lineage of {{ill|Tsushima Shrine|en|Tsushima Shrine|WD=Q705136|lt=Tsushima Shrine}} (Tsushima City, Aichi Prefecture). Other shrine names based on the Gion faith include {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|八坂神社 (曖昧さ回避)|lt=Yasaka Shrine|lt_ja=八坂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka Shrine (disambiguation)|qq=}} (also known as {{ill|Yaei Shrine|ja|弥栄神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yaei Shrine|1=Yaei Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487447}}), {{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Gion shrine|WD=Q11588442}}, {{ill|Hiromine Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|広峯神社 (曖昧さ回避)|lt=Hiromine Shrine|lt_ja=広峯神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiromine Shrine (disambiguation)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Yakumo Shrine|ja|八雲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakumo Shrine|1=Yakumo shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391847}}, {{ill|Suga Shrine|ja|須賀神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suga Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Susanoo Shrine|ja|素盞嗚神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo Shrine|qq=}}, names which vary according to the era and source.

Among the current enshrined deities, only a few shrines have {{ill|Gozu Tennō|en|Gozu Tennō|WD=Q11570247|lt=Gozu Tennō}} as their primary deity, while many shrines enshrine {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, who had been syncretized with Gozu Tennō through {{ill|Shinbutsu-shūgō|en|Shinbutsu-shūgō|WD=Q2279088|lt=Shinbutsu Shūgō}}. During the Meiji era's separation of Shinto and Buddhism ({{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|WD=Q1970535|lt=Shinbutsu Bunri}}), some shrines reverted to the deities recorded in ancient tradition rather than enshrining Susanoo.

Since the Meiji era, due to joint enshrinement ({{ill|Joint Enshrinement|ja|合祀|1=co-Enshrinement|2=ja|3=合祀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Joint Enshrinement|13=User:Immanuelle/co-Enshrinement|qq=}}) with other shrines and the renaming of institutions such as the Tsushima Gozu Tennō Shrine, many shrines have changed their names to, for example, Tsushima Shrine. Additionally, influenced by the separation of Shinto and Buddhism and such joint enshrinement, there are shrines where the enshrined deity is not {{ill|Gozu Tennō|en|Gozu Tennō|WD=Q11570247|lt=Gozu Tennō}}, and some where Gozu Tennō is interpreted as a deity other than Susanoo. In certain regions, the legacy of this phenomenon can even be seen in local place names bearing "Tennō."

__NOTOC__
'''Tenno Jinja''' (天王神社、天王社) is a Shinto shrine dedicated to {{ill|Gozu Tennō|en|Gozu Tennō|WD=Q11570247|lt=Gozu Tennō}} and {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, central deities in {{ill|Gion cult|en|Gion cult|WD=Q11588425|lt=Gion Faith}}. This name is especially used for shrines in the lineage of {{ill|Tsushima Shrine|en|Tsushima Shrine|WD=Q705136|lt=Tsushima Shrine}} in {{ill|Tsushima, Aichi|en|Tsushima, Aichi|WD=Q816736|lt=Tsushima City, Aichi Prefecture}}. Other shrines based on Gion Faith include {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|八坂神社 (曖昧さ回避)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka Shrine (disambiguation)|qq=}}, {{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Gion shrine|WD=Q11588442}}, {{ill|Hiromine Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|広峯神社 (曖昧さ回避)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiromine Shrine (disambiguation)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Yakumo Shrine|ja|八雲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakumo Shrine|1=Yakumo shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391847}}, {{ill|Suga Shrine|ja|須賀神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suga Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Susanoo Shrine|ja|素盞嗚神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo Shrine|qq=}}, whose usage varies by era and source.

Currently, only a few shrines still worship Gozu Tennō directly. Most shrines now enshrine {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, who was syncretized with Gozu Tennō under {{ill|Shinbutsu-shūgō|en|Shinbutsu-shūgō|WD=Q2279088|lt=Shinbutsu-shūgō}}. During the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|WD=Q1970535|lt=Shinbutsu bunri}} (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) in the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}}, some shrines reverted to deities listed in ancient records other than Susanoo.

Since the Meiji period, many shrines have been merged with others or renamed to align with the Tsushima Gozu Tennōsha, such as renaming to {{ill|Tsushima Shrine|en|Tsushima Shrine|WD=Q705136|lt=Tsushima Shrine}}. As a result of Shinbutsu bunri and shrine mergers, some shrines now enshrine deities other than Gozu Tennō or interpret Gozu Tennō as a deity distinct from Susanoo. In some regions, the influence of these shrines can still be seen in place names incorporating "Tenno."

== List ==
* {{ill|Tenno Shrine (Shichinohe)|ja|天王神社 (七戸町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenno Shrine (Shichinohe)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Aomori Prefecture, Kamikita District, Shichinohe, {{ill|Shichinohe|ja|七戸 (七戸町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichinohe|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Susanoo Shrine (Shibata)|ja|須佐之男神社 (新発田市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo Shrine (Shibata)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Tenno Shrine) - Niigata Prefecture, Shibata, {{ill|Tenno (Shibata)|ja|天王 (新発田市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenno (Shibata)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref>[http://www.niigata-kankou.or.jp/sys/data?page-id=10163], [http://www.niigata-u.com/files/ngt2009g/091010b1.html], [http://shibata-info.jp/archives/eventcal/%E5%A4%A9%E7%8E%8B%E3%81%BE%E3%81%A4%E3%82%8A]</ref>

* {{ill|Tenno Shrine Hachiman Shrine|ja|天王社八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenno Shrine Hachiman Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Tenno Shrine (Kizugawa)|ja|天王神社 (木津川市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenno Shrine (Kizugawa)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Kyoto Prefecture, Kizugawa, {{ill|Kizu Shimizu|ja|木津清水|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kizu Shimizu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

==Merged second translation==
__NOTOC__
'''Tennō Shrine''' (天王神社) and '''Tennō-sha''' (天王社) are Shinto shrines of the {{ill|Gion cult|en|Gion cult|WD=Q11588425|lt=Gion Faith}} tradition that enshrine {{ill|Gozu Tennō|en|Gozu Tennō|WD=Q11570247|lt=Gozu Tennō}} and {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}.

It is observed that this name is used in shrines belonging to the lineage of {{ill|Tsushima Shrine|en|Tsushima Shrine|WD=Q705136|lt=Tsushima Shrine}} (Tsushima City, Aichi Prefecture). Other shrine names based on the Gion faith include {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|八坂神社 (曖昧さ回避)|lt=Yasaka Shrine|lt_ja=八坂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka Shrine (disambiguation)|qq=}} (also known as {{ill|Yaei Shrine|ja|弥栄神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yaei Shrine|1=Yaei Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487447}}), {{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Gion shrine|WD=Q11588442}}, {{ill|Hiromine Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|広峯神社 (曖昧さ回避)|lt=Hiromine Shrine|lt_ja=広峯神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiromine Shrine (disambiguation)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Yakumo Shrine|ja|八雲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakumo Shrine|1=Yakumo shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391847}}, {{ill|Suga Shrine|ja|須賀神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suga Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Susanoo Shrine|ja|素盞嗚神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo Shrine|qq=}}, names which vary according to the era and source.

Among the current enshrined deities, only a few shrines have {{ill|Gozu Tennō|en|Gozu Tennō|WD=Q11570247|lt=Gozu Tennō}} as their primary deity, while many shrines enshrine {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, who had been syncretized with Gozu Tennō through {{ill|Shinbutsu-shūgō|en|Shinbutsu-shūgō|WD=Q2279088|lt=Shinbutsu Shūgō}}. During the Meiji era's separation of Shinto and Buddhism ({{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|WD=Q1970535|lt=Shinbutsu Bunri}}), some shrines reverted to the deities recorded in ancient tradition rather than enshrining Susanoo.

Since the Meiji era, due to joint enshrinement ({{ill|Joint Enshrinement|ja|合祀|1=co-Enshrinement|2=ja|3=合祀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Joint Enshrinement|13=User:Immanuelle/co-Enshrinement|qq=}}) with other shrines and the renaming of institutions such as the Tsushima Gozu Tennō Shrine, many shrines have changed their names to, for example, Tsushima Shrine. Additionally, influenced by the separation of Shinto and Buddhism and such joint enshrinement, there are shrines where the enshrined deity is not {{ill|Gozu Tennō|en|Gozu Tennō|WD=Q11570247|lt=Gozu Tennō}}, and some where Gozu Tennō is interpreted as a deity other than Susanoo. In certain regions, the legacy of this phenomenon can even be seen in local place names bearing "Tennō."

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:天王神社]]

__NOTOC__
'''Tenno Jinja''' (天王神社、天王社) is a Shinto shrine dedicated to {{ill|Gozu Tennō|en|Gozu Tennō|WD=Q11570247|lt=Gozu Tennō}} and {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, central deities in {{ill|Gion cult|en|Gion cult|WD=Q11588425|lt=Gion Faith}}. This name is especially used for shrines in the lineage of {{ill|Tsushima Shrine|en|Tsushima Shrine|WD=Q705136|lt=Tsushima Shrine}} in {{ill|Tsushima, Aichi|en|Tsushima, Aichi|WD=Q816736|lt=Tsushima City, Aichi Prefecture}}. Other shrines based on Gion Faith include {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|八坂神社 (曖昧さ回避)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka Shrine (disambiguation)|qq=}}, {{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Gion shrine|WD=Q11588442}}, {{ill|Hiromine Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|広峯神社 (曖昧さ回避)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiromine Shrine (disambiguation)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Yakumo Shrine|ja|八雲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakumo Shrine|1=Yakumo shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391847}}, {{ill|Suga Shrine|ja|須賀神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suga Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Susanoo Shrine|ja|素盞嗚神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo Shrine|qq=}}, whose usage varies by era and source.

Currently, only a few shrines still worship Gozu Tennō directly. Most shrines now enshrine {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, who was syncretized with Gozu Tennō under {{ill|Shinbutsu-shūgō|en|Shinbutsu-shūgō|WD=Q2279088|lt=Shinbutsu-shūgō}}. During the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|WD=Q1970535|lt=Shinbutsu bunri}} (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) in the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}}, some shrines reverted to deities listed in ancient records other than Susanoo.

Since the Meiji period, many shrines have been merged with others or renamed to align with the Tsushima Gozu Tennōsha, such as renaming to {{ill|Tsushima Shrine|en|Tsushima Shrine|WD=Q705136|lt=Tsushima Shrine}}. As a result of Shinbutsu bunri and shrine mergers, some shrines now enshrine deities other than Gozu Tennō or interpret Gozu Tennō as a deity distinct from Susanoo. In some regions, the influence of these shrines can still be seen in place names incorporating "Tenno."

* {{ill|Tenno Shrine (Kizugawa)|ja|天王神社 (木津川市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenno Shrine (Kizugawa)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Kyoto Prefecture, Kizugawa, {{ill|Kizu Shimizu|ja|木津清水|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kizu Shimizu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

'''Tennō Shrine''' (天王神社) and '''Tennō-sha''' (天王社) are Shinto shrines of the {{ill|Gion cult|en|Gion cult|WD=Q11588425|lt=Gion Faith}} tradition that enshrine {{ill|Gozu Tennō|en|Gozu Tennō|WD=Q11570247|lt=Gozu Tennō}} and {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}.

It is observed that this name is used in shrines belonging to the lineage of {{ill|Tsushima Shrine|en|Tsushima Shrine|WD=Q705136|lt=Tsushima Shrine}} (Tsushima City, Aichi Prefecture). Other shrine names based on the Gion faith include {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|八坂神社 (曖昧さ回避)|lt=Yasaka Shrine|lt_ja=八坂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka Shrine (disambiguation)|qq=}} (also known as {{ill|Yaei Shrine|ja|弥栄神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yaei Shrine|1=Yaei Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487447}}), {{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Gion shrine|WD=Q11588442}}, {{ill|Hiromine Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|広峯神社 (曖昧さ回避)|lt=Hiromine Shrine|lt_ja=広峯神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiromine Shrine (disambiguation)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Yakumo Shrine|ja|八雲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakumo Shrine|1=Yakumo shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391847}}, {{ill|Suga Shrine|ja|須賀神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suga Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Susanoo Shrine|ja|素盞嗚神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo Shrine|qq=}}, names which vary according to the era and source.

Among the current enshrined deities, only a few shrines have {{ill|Gozu Tennō|en|Gozu Tennō|WD=Q11570247|lt=Gozu Tennō}} as their primary deity, while many shrines enshrine {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, who had been syncretized with Gozu Tennō through {{ill|Shinbutsu-shūgō|en|Shinbutsu-shūgō|WD=Q2279088|lt=Shinbutsu Shūgō}}. During the Meiji era's separation of Shinto and Buddhism ({{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|WD=Q1970535|lt=Shinbutsu Bunri}}), some shrines reverted to the deities recorded in ancient tradition rather than enshrining Susanoo.

Since the Meiji era, due to joint enshrinement ({{ill|Joint Enshrinement|ja|合祀|1=co-Enshrinement|2=ja|3=合祀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Joint Enshrinement|13=User:Immanuelle/co-Enshrinement|qq=}}) with other shrines and the renaming of institutions such as the Tsushima Gozu Tennō Shrine, many shrines have changed their names to, for example, Tsushima Shrine. Additionally, influenced by the separation of Shinto and Buddhism and such joint enshrinement, there are shrines where the enshrined deity is not {{ill|Gozu Tennō|en|Gozu Tennō|WD=Q11570247|lt=Gozu Tennō}}, and some where Gozu Tennō is interpreted as a deity other than Susanoo. In certain regions, the legacy of this phenomenon can even be seen in local place names bearing "Tennō."

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:天王神社]]

__NOTOC__
'''Tenno Jinja''' (天王神社、天王社) is a Shinto shrine dedicated to {{ill|Gozu Tennō|en|Gozu Tennō|WD=Q11570247|lt=Gozu Tennō|1=Gozu Tennō}} and {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, central deities in {{ill|Gion cult|en|Gion cult|WD=Q11588425|lt=Gion Faith|1=Gion Faith}}. This name is especially used for shrines in the lineage of {{ill|Tsushima Shrine|en|Tsushima Shrine|WD=Q705136|lt=Tsushima Shrine|1=Tsushima Shrine}} in {{ill|Tsushima, Aichi|en|Tsushima, Aichi|WD=Q816736|lt=Tsushima City, Aichi Prefecture|1=Tsushima, Aichi}}. Other shrines based on Gion Faith include {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|八坂神社 (曖昧さ回避)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka Shrine (disambiguation)|qq=}}, {{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Gion shrines|WD=Q11588442}}, {{ill|Hiromine Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|広峯神社 (曖昧さ回避)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiromine Shrine (disambiguation)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Yakumo Shrine|ja|八雲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakumo Shrine|1=Yakumo shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391847}}, {{ill|Suga Shrine|ja|須賀神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suga Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Susanoo Shrine|ja|素盞嗚神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo Shrine|qq=}}, whose usage varies by era and source.

Currently, only a few shrines still worship Gozu Tennō directly. Most shrines now enshrine {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, who was syncretized with Gozu Tennō under {{ill|Shinbutsu-shūgō|en|Shinbutsu-shūgō|WD=Q2279088|lt=Shinbutsu-shūgō|1=Shinbutsu-shūgō}}. During the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|WD=Q1970535|lt=Shinbutsu bunri|1=Shinbutsu bunri}} (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) in the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}}, some shrines reverted to deities listed in ancient records other than Susanoo.

Since the Meiji period, many shrines have been merged with others or renamed to align with the Tsushima Gozu Tennōsha, such as renaming to {{ill|Tsushima Shrine|en|Tsushima Shrine|WD=Q705136|lt=Tsushima Shrine|1=Tsushima Shrine}}. As a result of Shinbutsu bunri and shrine mergers, some shrines now enshrine deities other than Gozu Tennō or interpret Gozu Tennō as a deity distinct from Susanoo. In some regions, the influence of these shrines can still be seen in place names incorporating "Tenno."

* {{ill|Tenno Shrine (Shichinohe)|ja|天王神社 (七戸町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenno Shrine (Shichinohe)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Aomori Prefecture, Kamikita District, Shichinohe, {{ill|Shichinohe|ja|七戸 (七戸町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichinohe|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Susanoo Shrine (Shibata)|ja|須佐之男神社 (新発田市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo Shrine (Shibata)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Tenno Shrine) - Niigata Prefecture, Shibata, {{ill|Tenno (Shibata)|ja|天王 (新発田市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenno (Shibata)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref>[http://www.niigata-kankou.or.jp/sys/data?page-id=10163], [http://www.niigata-u.com/files/ngt2009g/091010b1.html], [http://shibata-info.jp/archives/eventcal/%E5%A4%A9%E7%8E%8B%E3%81%BE%E3%81%A4%E3%82%8A]</ref>
* {{ill|Tenno Shrine (Kameoka)|ja|天王神社 (亀岡市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenno Shrine (Kameoka)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Kyoto Prefecture, Kameoka, {{ill|Miyamae Town|ja|宮前町 (亀岡市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamae Town|1=Miyazakichou|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q86733848}}, Kamisaki, Tenno Shita<ref>[http://agua.jpn.org/film/f99.html], [http://901-1000.wakkan.jp/927.html]</ref>
* {{ill|Tenno Shrine Hachiman Shrine|ja|天王社八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenno Shrine Hachiman Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Tenno Shrine (Kizugawa)|ja|天王神社 (木津川市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenno Shrine (Kizugawa)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Kyoto Prefecture, Kizugawa, {{ill|Kizu Shimizu|ja|木津清水|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kizu Shimizu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Tenno Shrine (Komatsushima)|ja|天王社 (小松島市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenno Shrine (Komatsushima)|qq=}} - Tokushima Prefecture, Komatsushima, {{ill|Tano Town (Komatsushima)|ja|田野町 (小松島市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tano Town (Komatsushima)|1=Tano, Komatsushima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577483}}
* {{ill|Gozu Tennō|en|Gozu Tennō|WD=Q11570247|lt=Gozu Tennō神社|1=Gozu Tennō}}
* {{ill|Tenno Hachiman Shrine|ja|天王八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenno Hachiman Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Kenoda Suga Shrine|ja|健田須賀神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenoda Suga Shrine|1=Kenda Suga Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11387225}}
* {{ill|Tennosha Shrine (Komatsushima City)|ja|天王社 (小松島市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tennosha Shrine (Komatsushima City)|qq=}}

During the Meiji era's separation of Shinto and Buddhism ({{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|WD=Q1970535|lt=Shinbutsu Bunri|1=Shinbutsu bunri}}), some shrines reverted to the deities recorded in ancient tradition rather than enshrining Susanoo.

Since the Meiji era, due to joint enshrinement ({{ill|Joint Enshrinement|ja|合祀|1=co-Enshrinement|2=ja|3=合祀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Joint Enshrinement|13=User:Immanuelle/co-Enshrinement|qq=}}) with other shrines and the renaming of institutions such as the Tsushima Gozu Tennō Shrine, many shrines have changed their names to, for example, Tsushima Shrine. Additionally, influenced by the separation of Shinto and Buddhism and such joint enshrinement, there are shrines where the enshrined deity is not {{ill|Gozu Tennō|en|Gozu Tennō|WD=Q11570247|lt=Gozu Tennō|1=Gozu Tennō}}, and some where Gozu Tennō is interpreted as a deity other than Susanoo. In certain regions, the legacy of this phenomenon can even be seen in local place names bearing "Tennō."

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:天王神社]]
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:天王神社]]

__NOTOC__
'''Tenno Jinja''' (天王神社、天王社) is a Shinto shrine dedicated to {{ill|Gozu Tennō|en|Gozu Tennō|WD=Q11570247|lt=Gozu Tennō|1=Gozu Tennō}} and {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, central deities in {{ill|Gion cult|en|Gion cult|WD=Q11588425|lt=Gion Faith|1=Gion Faith}}. This name is especially used for shrines in the lineage of {{ill|Tsushima Shrine|en|Tsushima Shrine|WD=Q705136|lt=Tsushima Shrine|1=Tsushima Shrine}} in {{ill|Tsushima, Aichi|en|Tsushima, Aichi|WD=Q816736|lt=Tsushima City, Aichi Prefecture|1=Tsushima, Aichi}}. Other shrines based on Gion Faith include {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|八坂神社 (曖昧さ回避)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka Shrine (disambiguation)|qq=}}, {{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Gion shrines|WD=Q11588442}}, {{ill|Hiromine Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|広峯神社 (曖昧さ回避)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiromine Shrine (disambiguation)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Yakumo Shrine|ja|八雲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakumo Shrine|1=Yakumo shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391847}}, {{ill|Suga Shrine|ja|須賀神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suga Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Susanoo Shrine|ja|素盞嗚神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo Shrine|qq=}}, whose usage varies by era and source.

Currently, only a few shrines still worship Gozu Tennō directly. Most shrines now enshrine {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, who was syncretized with Gozu Tennō under {{ill|Shinbutsu-shūgō|en|Shinbutsu-shūgō|WD=Q2279088|lt=Shinbutsu-shūgō|1=Shinbutsu-shūgō}}. During the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|WD=Q1970535|lt=Shinbutsu bunri|1=Shinbutsu bunri}} (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) in the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}}, some shrines reverted to deities listed in ancient records other than Susanoo.

Since the Meiji period, many shrines have been merged with others or renamed to align with the Tsushima Gozu Tennōsha, such as renaming to {{ill|Tsushima Shrine|en|Tsushima Shrine|WD=Q705136|lt=Tsushima Shrine|1=Tsushima Shrine}}. As a result of Shinbutsu bunri and shrine mergers, some shrines now enshrine deities other than Gozu Tennō or interpret Gozu Tennō as a deity distinct from Susanoo. In some regions, the influence of these shrines can still be seen in place names incorporating "Tenno."

* {{ill|Tenno Shrine (Shichinohe)|ja|天王神社 (七戸町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenno Shrine (Shichinohe)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Aomori Prefecture, Kamikita District, Shichinohe, {{ill|Shichinohe|ja|七戸 (七戸町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichinohe|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Susanoo Shrine (Shibata)|ja|須佐之男神社 (新発田市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo Shrine (Shibata)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Tenno Shrine) - Niigata Prefecture, Shibata, {{ill|Tenno (Shibata)|ja|天王 (新発田市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenno (Shibata)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref>[http://www.niigata-kankou.or.jp/sys/data?page-id=10163], [http://www.niigata-u.com/files/ngt2009g/091010b1.html], [http://shibata-info.jp/archives/eventcal/%E5%A4%A9%E7%8E%8B%E3%81%BE%E3%81%A4%E3%82%8A]</ref>

* {{ill|Tenno Shrine Hachiman Shrine|ja|天王社八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenno Shrine Hachiman Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Tenno Shrine (Kizugawa)|ja|天王神社 (木津川市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenno Shrine (Kizugawa)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Kyoto Prefecture, Kizugawa, {{ill|Kizu Shimizu|ja|木津清水|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kizu Shimizu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

* {{ill|Tenno Hachiman Shrine|ja|天王八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenno Hachiman Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Kenoda Suga Shrine|ja|健田須賀神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenoda Suga Shrine|1=Kenda Suga Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11387225}}

==Merged second translation==
__NOTOC__
'''Tennō Shrine''' (天王神社) and '''Tennō-sha''' (天王社) are Shinto shrines of the {{ill|Gion cult|en|Gion cult|WD=Q11588425|lt=Gion Faith|1=Gion Faith}} tradition that enshrine {{ill|Gozu Tennō|en|Gozu Tennō|WD=Q11570247|lt=Gozu Tennō|1=Gozu Tennō}} and {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}.

It is observed that this name is used in shrines belonging to the lineage of {{ill|Tsushima Shrine|en|Tsushima Shrine|WD=Q705136|lt=Tsushima Shrine|1=Tsushima Shrine}} (Tsushima City, Aichi Prefecture). Other shrine names based on the Gion faith include {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|八坂神社 (曖昧さ回避)|lt=Yasaka Shrine|lt_ja=八坂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka Shrine (disambiguation)|qq=}} (also known as {{ill|Yaei Shrine|ja|弥栄神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yaei Shrine|1=Q11487447|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487447}}), {{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Gion shrines|WD=Q11588442}}, {{ill|Hiromine Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|広峯神社 (曖昧さ回避)|lt=Hiromine Shrine|lt_ja=広峯神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiromine Shrine (disambiguation)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Yakumo Shrine|ja|八雲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakumo Shrine|1=Yakumo shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391847}}, {{ill|Suga Shrine|ja|須賀神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suga Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Susanoo Shrine|ja|素盞嗚神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo Shrine|qq=}}, names which vary according to the era and source.

Among the current enshrined deities, only a few shrines have {{ill|Gozu Tennō|en|Gozu Tennō|WD=Q11570247|lt=Gozu Tennō|1=Gozu Tennō}} as their primary deity, while many shrines enshrine {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, who had been syncretized with Gozu Tennō through {{ill|Shinbutsu-shūgō|en|Shinbutsu-shūgō|WD=Q2279088|lt=Shinbutsu Shūgō|1=Shinbutsu-shūgō}}. During the Meiji era's separation of Shinto and Buddhism ({{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|WD=Q1970535|lt=Shinbutsu Bunri|1=Shinbutsu bunri}}), some shrines reverted to the deities recorded in ancient tradition rather than enshrining Susanoo.

Since the Meiji era, due to joint enshrinement ({{ill|Joint Enshrinement|ja|合祀|1=co-Enshrinement|2=ja|3=合祀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Joint Enshrinement|13=User:Immanuelle/co-Enshrinement|qq=}}) with other shrines and the renaming of institutions such as the Tsushima Gozu Tennō Shrine, many shrines have changed their names to, for example, Tsushima Shrine. Additionally, influenced by the separation of Shinto and Buddhism and such joint enshrinement, there are shrines where the enshrined deity is not {{ill|Gozu Tennō|en|Gozu Tennō|WD=Q11570247|lt=Gozu Tennō|1=Gozu Tennō}}, and some where Gozu Tennō is interpreted as a deity other than Susanoo. In certain regions, the legacy of this phenomenon can even be seen in local place names bearing "Tennō."

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:天王神社]]
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:天王神社]]
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:天王神社]]

__NOTOC__
'''Tenno Jinja''' (天王神社、天王社) is a Shinto shrine dedicated to {{ill|Gozu Tennō|en|Gozu Tennō|WD=Q11570247|lt=Gozu Tennō|1=Gozu Tennō}} and {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, central deities in {{ill|Gion cult|en|Gion cult|WD=Q11588425|lt=Gion Faith|1=Gion Faith}}. This name is especially used for shrines in the lineage of {{ill|Tsushima Shrine|en|Tsushima Shrine|WD=Q705136|lt=Tsushima Shrine|1=Tsushima Shrine}} in {{ill|Tsushima, Aichi|en|Tsushima, Aichi|WD=Q816736|lt=Tsushima City, Aichi Prefecture|1=Tsushima, Aichi}}. Other shrines based on Gion Faith include {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|八坂神社 (曖昧さ回避)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka Shrine (disambiguation)|qq=}}, {{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Gion shrines|WD=Q11588442}}, {{ill|Hiromine Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|広峯神社 (曖昧さ回避)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiromine Shrine (disambiguation)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Yakumo Shrine|ja|八雲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakumo Shrine|1=Yakumo shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391847}}, {{ill|Suga Shrine|ja|須賀神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suga Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Susanoo Shrine|ja|素盞嗚神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo Shrine|qq=}}, whose usage varies by era and source.

Currently, only a few shrines still worship Gozu Tennō directly. Most shrines now enshrine {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, who was syncretized with Gozu Tennō under {{ill|Shinbutsu-shūgō|en|Shinbutsu-shūgō|WD=Q2279088|lt=Shinbutsu-shūgō|1=Shinbutsu-shūgō}}. During the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|WD=Q1970535|lt=Shinbutsu bunri|1=Shinbutsu bunri}} (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) in the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}}, some shrines reverted to deities listed in ancient records other than Susanoo.

Since the Meiji period, many shrines have been merged with others or renamed to align with the Tsushima Gozu Tennōsha, such as renaming to {{ill|Tsushima Shrine|en|Tsushima Shrine|WD=Q705136|lt=Tsushima Shrine|1=Tsushima Shrine}}. As a result of Shinbutsu bunri and shrine mergers, some shrines now enshrine deities other than Gozu Tennō or interpret Gozu Tennō as a deity distinct from Susanoo. In some regions, the influence of these shrines can still be seen in place names incorporating "Tenno."

* {{ill|Susanoo Shrine (Shibata)|ja|須佐之男神社 (新発田市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo Shrine (Shibata)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Tenno Shrine) - Niigata Prefecture, Shibata, {{ill|Tenno (Shibata)|ja|天王 (新発田市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenno (Shibata)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref>[http://www.niigata-kankou.or.jp/sys/data?page-id=10163], [http://www.niigata-u.com/files/ngt2009g/091010b1.html], [http://shibata-info.jp/archives/eventcal/%E5%A4%A9%E7%8E%8B%E3%81%BE%E3%81%A4%E3%82%8A]</ref>

* {{ill|Tenno Shrine (Kizugawa)|ja|天王神社 (木津川市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenno Shrine (Kizugawa)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Kyoto Prefecture, Kizugawa, {{ill|Kizu Shimizu|ja|木津清水|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kizu Shimizu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

==Merged second translation==
__NOTOC__
'''Tennō Shrine''' (天王神社) and '''Tennō-sha''' (天王社) are Shinto shrines of the {{ill|Gion cult|en|Gion cult|WD=Q11588425|lt=Gion Faith|1=Gion Faith}} tradition that enshrine {{ill|Gozu Tennō|en|Gozu Tennō|WD=Q11570247|lt=Gozu Tennō|1=Gozu Tennō}} and {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}.

It is observed that this name is used in shrines belonging to the lineage of {{ill|Tsushima Shrine|en|Tsushima Shrine|WD=Q705136|lt=Tsushima Shrine|1=Tsushima Shrine}} (Tsushima City, Aichi Prefecture). Other shrine names based on the Gion faith include {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|八坂神社 (曖昧さ回避)|lt=Yasaka Shrine|lt_ja=八坂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka Shrine (disambiguation)|qq=}} (also known as {{ill|Yaei Shrine|ja|弥栄神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yaei Shrine|1=Q11487447|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487447}}), {{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Gion shrines|WD=Q11588442}}, {{ill|Hiromine Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|広峯神社 (曖昧さ回避)|lt=Hiromine Shrine|lt_ja=広峯神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiromine Shrine (disambiguation)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Yakumo Shrine|ja|八雲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakumo Shrine|1=Yakumo shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391847}}, {{ill|Suga Shrine|ja|須賀神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suga Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Susanoo Shrine|ja|素盞嗚神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo Shrine|qq=}}, names which vary according to the era and source.

Among the current enshrined deities, only a few shrines have {{ill|Gozu Tennō|en|Gozu Tennō|WD=Q11570247|lt=Gozu Tennō|1=Gozu Tennō}} as their primary deity, while many shrines enshrine {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, who had been syncretized with Gozu Tennō through {{ill|Shinbutsu-shūgō|en|Shinbutsu-shūgō|WD=Q2279088|lt=Shinbutsu Shūgō|1=Shinbutsu-shūgō}}. During the Meiji era's separation of Shinto and Buddhism ({{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|WD=Q1970535|lt=Shinbutsu Bunri|1=Shinbutsu bunri}}), some shrines reverted to the deities recorded in ancient tradition rather than enshrining Susanoo.

Since the Meiji era, due to joint enshrinement ({{ill|Joint Enshrinement|ja|合祀|1=co-Enshrinement|2=ja|3=合祀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Joint Enshrinement|13=User:Immanuelle/co-Enshrinement|qq=}}) with other shrines and the renaming of institutions such as the Tsushima Gozu Tennō Shrine, many shrines have changed their names to, for example, Tsushima Shrine. Additionally, influenced by the separation of Shinto and Buddhism and such joint enshrinement, there are shrines where the enshrined deity is not {{ill|Gozu Tennō|en|Gozu Tennō|WD=Q11570247|lt=Gozu Tennō|1=Gozu Tennō}}, and some where Gozu Tennō is interpreted as a deity other than Susanoo. In certain regions, the legacy of this phenomenon can even be seen in local place names bearing "Tennō."

== Main Tennō Shrines ==
* {{ill|Tennō Shrine (Shichinohe-machi)|ja|天王神社 (七戸町)|lt=Tennō Shrine|lt_ja=天王神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tennō Shrine (Shichinohe-machi)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|en|Aomori Prefecture|WD=Q71699|lt=Aomori Prefecture|1=Aomori Prefecture}}{{ill|Kamikita District, Aomori|en|Kamikita District, Aomori|WD=Q1143970|lt=Kamikita District|1=Kamikita District, Aomori}}{{ill|Shichinohe|en|Shichinohe|WD=Q1349448|lt=Shichinohe Town|1=Shichinohe}} Shichinohe

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:天王神社]]

__NOTOC__
'''Tenno Jinja''' (天王神社、天王社) is a Shinto shrine dedicated to {{ill|Gozu Tennō|en|Gozu Tennō|WD=Q11570247|lt=Gozu Tennō|1=Gozu Tennō}} and {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, central deities in {{ill|Gion cult|en|Gion cult|WD=Q11588425|lt=Gion Faith|1=Gion Faith}}. This name is especially used for shrines in the lineage of {{ill|Tsushima Shrine|en|Tsushima Shrine|WD=Q705136|lt=Tsushima Shrine|1=Tsushima Shrine}} in {{ill|Tsushima, Aichi|en|Tsushima, Aichi|WD=Q816736|lt=Tsushima City, Aichi Prefecture|1=Tsushima, Aichi}}. Other shrines based on Gion Faith include {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|八坂神社 (曖昧さ回避)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka Shrine (disambiguation)|qq=}}, {{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Gion shrines|WD=Q11588442}}, {{ill|Hiromine Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|広峯神社 (曖昧さ回避)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiromine Shrine (disambiguation)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Yakumo Shrine|ja|八雲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakumo Shrine|1=Yakumo shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391847}}, {{ill|Suga Shrine|ja|須賀神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suga Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Susanoo Shrine|ja|素盞嗚神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo Shrine|qq=}}, whose usage varies by era and source.

Currently, only a few shrines still worship Gozu Tennō directly. Most shrines now enshrine {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, who was syncretized with Gozu Tennō under {{ill|Shinbutsu-shūgō|en|Shinbutsu-shūgō|WD=Q2279088|lt=Shinbutsu-shūgō|1=Shinbutsu-shūgō}}. During the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|WD=Q1970535|lt=Shinbutsu bunri|1=Shinbutsu bunri}} (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) in the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}}, some shrines reverted to deities listed in ancient records other than Susanoo.

Since the Meiji period, many shrines have been merged with others or renamed to align with the Tsushima Gozu Tennōsha, such as renaming to {{ill|Tsushima Shrine|en|Tsushima Shrine|WD=Q705136|lt=Tsushima Shrine|1=Tsushima Shrine}}. As a result of Shinbutsu bunri and shrine mergers, some shrines now enshrine deities other than Gozu Tennō or interpret Gozu Tennō as a deity distinct from Susanoo. In some regions, the influence of these shrines can still be seen in place names incorporating "Tenno."

* {{ill|Susanoo Shrine (Shibata)|ja|須佐之男神社 (新発田市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo Shrine (Shibata)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Tenno Shrine) - Niigata Prefecture, Shibata, {{ill|Tenno (Shibata)|ja|天王 (新発田市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenno (Shibata)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref>[http://www.niigata-kankou.or.jp/sys/data?page-id=10163], [http://www.niigata-u.com/files/ngt2009g/091010b1.html], [http://shibata-info.jp/archives/eventcal/%E5%A4%A9%E7%8E%8B%E3%81%BE%E3%81%A4%E3%82%8A]</ref>

* {{ill|Tenno Shrine (Kizugawa)|ja|天王神社 (木津川市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenno Shrine (Kizugawa)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Kyoto Prefecture, Kizugawa, {{ill|Kizu Shimizu|ja|木津清水|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kizu Shimizu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

==Merged second translation==
__NOTOC__
'''Tennō Shrine''' (天王神社) and '''Tennō-sha''' (天王社) are Shinto shrines of the {{ill|Gion cult|en|Gion cult|WD=Q11588425|lt=Gion Faith|1=Gion Faith}} tradition that enshrine {{ill|Gozu Tennō|en|Gozu Tennō|WD=Q11570247|lt=Gozu Tennō|1=Gozu Tennō}} and {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}.

It is observed that this name is used in shrines belonging to the lineage of {{ill|Tsushima Shrine|en|Tsushima Shrine|WD=Q705136|lt=Tsushima Shrine|1=Tsushima Shrine}} (Tsushima City, Aichi Prefecture). Other shrine names based on the Gion faith include {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|八坂神社 (曖昧さ回避)|lt=Yasaka Shrine|lt_ja=八坂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka Shrine (disambiguation)|qq=}} (also known as {{ill|Yaei Shrine|ja|弥栄神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yaei Shrine|1=Q11487447|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487447}}), {{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Gion shrines|WD=Q11588442}}, {{ill|Hiromine Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|広峯神社 (曖昧さ回避)|lt=Hiromine Shrine|lt_ja=広峯神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiromine Shrine (disambiguation)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Yakumo Shrine|ja|八雲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakumo Shrine|1=Yakumo shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391847}}, {{ill|Suga Shrine|ja|須賀神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suga Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Susanoo Shrine|ja|素盞嗚神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo Shrine|qq=}}, names which vary according to the era and source.

Among the current enshrined deities, only a few shrines have {{ill|Gozu Tennō|en|Gozu Tennō|WD=Q11570247|lt=Gozu Tennō|1=Gozu Tennō}} as their primary deity, while many shrines enshrine {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, who had been syncretized with Gozu Tennō through {{ill|Shinbutsu-shūgō|en|Shinbutsu-shūgō|WD=Q2279088|lt=Shinbutsu Shūgō|1=Shinbutsu-shūgō}}. During the Meiji era's separation of Shinto and Buddhism ({{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|WD=Q1970535|lt=Shinbutsu Bunri|1=Shinbutsu bunri}}), some shrines reverted to the deities recorded in ancient tradition rather than enshrining Susanoo.

Since the Meiji era, due to joint enshrinement ({{ill|Joint Enshrinement|ja|合祀|1=co-Enshrinement|2=ja|3=合祀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Joint Enshrinement|13=User:Immanuelle/co-Enshrinement|qq=}}) with other shrines and the renaming of institutions such as the Tsushima Gozu Tennō Shrine, many shrines have changed their names to, for example, Tsushima Shrine. Additionally, influenced by the separation of Shinto and Buddhism and such joint enshrinement, there are shrines where the enshrined deity is not {{ill|Gozu Tennō|en|Gozu Tennō|WD=Q11570247|lt=Gozu Tennō|1=Gozu Tennō}}, and some where Gozu Tennō is interpreted as a deity other than Susanoo. In certain regions, the legacy of this phenomenon can even be seen in local place names bearing "Tennō."

* {{ill|Gozu Tennō|en|Gozu Tennō|WD=Q11570247|lt=Gozu Tennōsha|1=Gozu Tennō}} – {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo Metropolis|1=Tokyo}}{{ill|Katsushika|en|Katsushika|WD=Q232628|lt=Katsushika|1=Katsushika}}{{ill|Okuto|ja|奥戸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okuto|1=Okudo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11445809}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:天王神社]]

__NOTOC__
'''Tenno Jinja''' (天王神社、天王社) is a Shinto shrine dedicated to {{ill|Gozu Tennō|en|Gozu Tennō|WD=Q11570247|lt=Gozu Tennō|1=Gozu Tennō}} and {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, central deities in {{ill|Gion cult|en|Gion cult|WD=Q11588425|lt=Gion Faith|1=Gion Faith}}. This name is especially used for shrines in the lineage of {{ill|Tsushima Shrine|en|Tsushima Shrine|WD=Q705136|lt=Tsushima Shrine|1=Tsushima Shrine}} in {{ill|Tsushima, Aichi|en|Tsushima, Aichi|WD=Q816736|lt=Tsushima City, Aichi Prefecture|1=Tsushima, Aichi}}. Other shrines based on Gion Faith include {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|八坂神社 (曖昧さ回避)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka Shrine (disambiguation)|qq=}}, {{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Gion shrines|WD=Q11588442}}, {{ill|Hiromine Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|広峯神社 (曖昧さ回避)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiromine Shrine (disambiguation)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Yakumo Shrine|ja|八雲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakumo Shrine|1=Yakumo shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391847}}, {{ill|Suga Shrine|ja|須賀神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suga Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Susanoo Shrine|ja|素盞嗚神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo Shrine|qq=}}, whose usage varies by era and source.

Currently, only a few shrines still worship Gozu Tennō directly. Most shrines now enshrine {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, who was syncretized with Gozu Tennō under {{ill|Shinbutsu-shūgō|en|Shinbutsu-shūgō|WD=Q2279088|lt=Shinbutsu-shūgō|1=Shinbutsu-shūgō}}. During the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|WD=Q1970535|lt=Shinbutsu bunri|1=Shinbutsu bunri}} (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) in the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}}, some shrines reverted to deities listed in ancient records other than Susanoo.

Since the Meiji period, many shrines have been merged with others or renamed to align with the Tsushima Gozu Tennōsha, such as renaming to {{ill|Tsushima Shrine|en|Tsushima Shrine|WD=Q705136|lt=Tsushima Shrine|1=Tsushima Shrine}}. As a result of Shinbutsu bunri and shrine mergers, some shrines now enshrine deities other than Gozu Tennō or interpret Gozu Tennō as a deity distinct from Susanoo. In some regions, the influence of these shrines can still be seen in place names incorporating "Tenno."

* {{ill|Susanoo Shrine (Shibata)|ja|須佐之男神社 (新発田市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo Shrine (Shibata)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Tenno Shrine) - Niigata Prefecture, Shibata, {{ill|Tenno (Shibata)|ja|天王 (新発田市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenno (Shibata)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref>[http://www.niigata-kankou.or.jp/sys/data?page-id=10163], [http://www.niigata-u.com/files/ngt2009g/091010b1.html], [http://shibata-info.jp/archives/eventcal/%E5%A4%A9%E7%8E%8B%E3%81%BE%E3%81%A4%E3%82%8A]</ref>

* {{ill|Tenno Shrine (Kizugawa)|ja|天王神社 (木津川市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenno Shrine (Kizugawa)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Kyoto Prefecture, Kizugawa, {{ill|Kizu Shimizu|ja|木津清水|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kizu Shimizu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

==Merged second translation==
__NOTOC__
'''Tennō Shrine''' (天王神社) and '''Tennō-sha''' (天王社) are Shinto shrines of the {{ill|Gion cult|en|Gion cult|WD=Q11588425|lt=Gion Faith|1=Gion Faith}} tradition that enshrine {{ill|Gozu Tennō|en|Gozu Tennō|WD=Q11570247|lt=Gozu Tennō|1=Gozu Tennō}} and {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}.

It is observed that this name is used in shrines belonging to the lineage of {{ill|Tsushima Shrine|en|Tsushima Shrine|WD=Q705136|lt=Tsushima Shrine|1=Tsushima Shrine}} (Tsushima City, Aichi Prefecture). Other shrine names based on the Gion faith include {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|八坂神社 (曖昧さ回避)|lt=Yasaka Shrine|lt_ja=八坂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka Shrine (disambiguation)|qq=}} (also known as {{ill|Yaei Shrine|ja|弥栄神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yaei Shrine|1=Q11487447|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487447}}), {{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Gion shrines|WD=Q11588442}}, {{ill|Hiromine Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|広峯神社 (曖昧さ回避)|lt=Hiromine Shrine|lt_ja=広峯神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiromine Shrine (disambiguation)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Yakumo Shrine|ja|八雲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakumo Shrine|1=Yakumo shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391847}}, {{ill|Suga Shrine|ja|須賀神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suga Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Susanoo Shrine|ja|素盞嗚神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo Shrine|qq=}}, names which vary according to the era and source.

Among the current enshrined deities, only a few shrines have {{ill|Gozu Tennō|en|Gozu Tennō|WD=Q11570247|lt=Gozu Tennō|1=Gozu Tennō}} as their primary deity, while many shrines enshrine {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, who had been syncretized with Gozu Tennō through {{ill|Shinbutsu-shūgō|en|Shinbutsu-shūgō|WD=Q2279088|lt=Shinbutsu Shūgō|1=Shinbutsu-shūgō}}. During the Meiji era's separation of Shinto and Buddhism ({{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|WD=Q1970535|lt=Shinbutsu Bunri|1=Shinbutsu bunri}}), some shrines reverted to the deities recorded in ancient tradition rather than enshrining Susanoo.

Since the Meiji era, due to joint enshrinement ({{ill|Joint Enshrinement|ja|合祀|1=co-Enshrinement|2=ja|3=合祀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Joint Enshrinement|13=User:Immanuelle/co-Enshrinement|qq=}}) with other shrines and the renaming of institutions such as the Tsushima Gozu Tennō Shrine, many shrines have changed their names to, for example, Tsushima Shrine. Additionally, influenced by the separation of Shinto and Buddhism and such joint enshrinement, there are shrines where the enshrined deity is not {{ill|Gozu Tennō|en|Gozu Tennō|WD=Q11570247|lt=Gozu Tennō|1=Gozu Tennō}}, and some where Gozu Tennō is interpreted as a deity other than Susanoo. In certain regions, the legacy of this phenomenon can even be seen in local place names bearing "Tennō."
